entries.label: ACP
entries.help: ACP runtime controls for enabling dispatch, selecting backends, constraining allowed agent targets, and tuning streamed turn projection behavior.

entries.label: ACP Allowed Agents
entries.help: Allowlist of ACP target agent ids permitted for ACP runtime sessions. Empty means no additional allowlist restriction.

entries.label: ACP Backend
entries.help: Default ACP runtime backend id (for example: acpx). Must match a registered ACP runtime plugin backend.

entries.label: ACP Default Agent
entries.help: Fallback ACP target agent id used when ACP spawns do not specify an explicit target.

entries.label: ACP Dispatch Enabled
entries.help: Independent dispatch gate for ACP session turns (default: true). Set false to keep ACP commands available while blocking ACP turn execution.

entries.label: ACP Enabled
entries.help: Global ACP feature gate. Keep disabled unless ACP runtime + policy are configured.

entries.label: ACP Max Concurrent Sessions
entries.help: Maximum concurrently active ACP sessions across this gateway process.

entries.label: ACP Runtime Install Command
entries.help: Optional operator install/setup command shown by `/acp install` and `/acp doctor` when ACP backend wiring is missing.

entries.label: ACP Runtime TTL (minutes)
entries.help: Idle runtime TTL in minutes for ACP session workers before eligible cleanup.

entries.label: ACP Stream
entries.help: ACP streaming projection controls for chunk sizing, metadata visibility, and deduped delivery behavior.

entries.label: ACP Stream Coalesce Idle (ms)
entries.help: Coalescer idle flush window in milliseconds for ACP streamed text before block replies are emitted.

entries.label: ACP Stream Delivery Mode
entries.help: ACP delivery style: live streams projected output incrementally, final_only buffers all projected ACP output until terminal turn events.

entries.label: ACP Stream Hidden Boundary Separator
entries.help: Separator inserted before next visible assistant text when hidden ACP tool lifecycle events occurred (none|space|newline|paragraph). Default: paragraph.

entries.label: ACP Stream Max Chunk Chars
entries.help: Maximum chunk size for ACP streamed block projection before splitting into multiple block replies.

entries.label: ACP Stream Max Output Chars
entries.help: Maximum assistant output characters projected per ACP turn before truncation notice is emitted.

entries.label: ACP Stream Max Session Update Chars
entries.help: Maximum characters for projected ACP session/update lines (tool/status updates).

entries.label: ACP Stream Repeat Suppression
entries.help: When true (default), suppress repeated ACP status/tool projection lines in a turn while keeping raw ACP events unchanged.

entries.label: ACP Stream Tag Visibility
entries.help: Per-sessionUpdate visibility overrides for ACP projection (for example usage_update, available_commands_update).

entries.label: Agents
entries.help: Agent runtime configuration root covering defaults and explicit agent entries used for routing and execution context. Keep this section explicit so model/tool behavior stays predictable across multi-agent workflows.

entries.label: Agent Defaults
entries.help: Shared default settings inherited by agents unless overridden per entry in agents.list. Use defaults to enforce consistent baseline behavior and reduce duplicated per-agent configuration.

entries.label: Bootstrap Max Chars
entries.help: Max characters of each workspace bootstrap file injected into the system prompt before truncation (default: 20000).

entries.label: Bootstrap Prompt Truncation Warning
entries.help: Inject agent-visible warning text when bootstrap files are truncated: "off", "once" (default), or "always".

entries.label: Bootstrap Total Max Chars
entries.help: Max total characters across all injected workspace bootstrap files (default: 150000).

entries.label: CLI Backends
entries.help: Optional CLI backends for text-only fallback (claude-cli, etc.).

entries.label: Compaction
entries.help: Compaction tuning for when context nears token limits, including history share, reserve headroom, and pre-compaction memory flush behavior. Use this when long-running sessions need stable continuity under tight context windows.

entries.label: Compaction Identifier Instructions
entries.help: Custom identifier-preservation instruction text used when identifierPolicy="custom". Keep this explicit and safety-focused so compaction summaries do not rewrite opaque IDs, URLs, hosts, or ports.

entries.label: Compaction Identifier Policy
entries.help: Identifier-preservation policy for compaction summaries: "strict" prepends built-in opaque-identifier retention guidance (default), "off" disables this prefix, and "custom" uses identifierInstructions. Keep "strict" unless you have a specific compatibility need.

entries.label: Compaction Keep Recent Tokens
entries.help: Minimum token budget preserved from the most recent conversation window during compaction. Use higher values to protect immediate context continuity and lower values to keep more long-tail history.

entries.label: Compaction Max History Share
entries.help: Maximum fraction of total context budget allowed for retained history after compaction (range 0.1-0.9). Use lower shares for more generation headroom or higher shares for deeper historical continuity.

entries.label: Compaction Memory Flush
entries.help: Pre-compaction memory flush settings that run an agentic memory write before heavy compaction. Keep enabled for long sessions so salient context is persisted before aggressive trimming.

entries.label: Compaction Memory Flush Enabled
entries.help: Enables pre-compaction memory flush before the runtime performs stronger history reduction near token limits. Keep enabled unless you intentionally disable memory side effects in constrained environments.

entries.label: Compaction Memory Flush Transcript Size Threshold
entries.help: Forces pre-compaction memory flush when transcript file size reaches this threshold (bytes or strings like "2mb"). Use this to prevent long-session hangs even when token counters are stale; set to 0 to disable.

entries.label: Compaction Memory Flush Prompt
entries.help: User-prompt template used for the pre-compaction memory flush turn when generating memory candidates. Use this only when you need custom extraction instructions beyond the default memory flush behavior.

entries.label: Compaction Memory Flush Soft Threshold
entries.help: Threshold distance to compaction (in tokens) that triggers pre-compaction memory flush execution. Use earlier thresholds for safer persistence, or tighter thresholds for lower flush frequency.

entries.label: Compaction Memory Flush System Prompt
entries.help: System-prompt override for the pre-compaction memory flush turn to control extraction style and safety constraints. Use carefully so custom instructions do not reduce memory quality or leak sensitive context.

entries.label: Compaction Mode
entries.help: Compaction strategy mode: "default" uses baseline behavior, while "safeguard" applies stricter guardrails to preserve recent context. Keep "default" unless you observe aggressive history loss near limit boundaries.

entries.label: Compaction Model Override
entries.help: Optional provider/model override used only for compaction summarization. Set this when you want compaction to run on a different model than the session default, and leave it unset to keep using the primary agent model.

entries.label: Compaction Notify User
entries.help: When enabled, sends a brief compaction notice to the user (e.g. '🧹 Compacting context...') when compaction starts. Disabled by default to keep compaction silent and non-intrusive.

entries.label: Post-Compaction Context Sections
entries.help: AGENTS.md H2/H3 section names re-injected after compaction so the agent reruns critical startup guidance. Leave unset to use "Session Startup"/"Red Lines" with legacy fallback to "Every Session"/"Safety"; set to [] to disable reinjection entirely.

entries.label: Compaction Post-Index Sync
entries.help: Controls post-compaction session memory reindex mode: "off", "async", or "await" (default: "async"). Use "await" for strongest freshness, "async" for lower compaction latency, and "off" only when session-memory sync is handled elsewhere.

entries.label: Compaction Quality Guard
entries.help: Optional quality-audit retry settings for safeguard compaction summaries. Leave this disabled unless you explicitly want summary audits and one-shot regeneration on failed checks.

entries.label: Compaction Quality Guard Enabled
entries.help: Enables summary quality audits and regeneration retries for safeguard compaction. Default: false, so safeguard mode alone does not turn on retry behavior.

entries.label: Compaction Quality Guard Max Retries
entries.help: Maximum number of regeneration retries after a failed safeguard summary quality audit. Use small values to bound extra latency and token cost.

entries.label: Compaction Preserve Recent Turns
entries.help: Number of most recent user/assistant turns kept verbatim outside safeguard summarization (default: 3). Raise this to preserve exact recent dialogue context, or lower it to maximize compaction savings.

entries.label: Compaction Reserve Tokens
entries.help: Token headroom reserved for reply generation and tool output after compaction runs. Use higher reserves for verbose/tool-heavy sessions, and lower reserves when maximizing retained history matters more.

entries.label: Compaction Reserve Token Floor
entries.help: Minimum floor enforced for reserveTokens in Pi compaction paths (0 disables the floor guard). Use a non-zero floor to avoid over-aggressive compression under fluctuating token estimates.

entries.label: Compaction Timeout (Seconds)
entries.help: Maximum time in seconds allowed for a single compaction operation before it is aborted (default: 900). Increase this for very large sessions that need more time to summarize, or decrease it to fail faster on unresponsive models.

entries.label: Embedded Pi
entries.help: Embedded Pi runner hardening controls for how workspace-local Pi settings are trusted and applied in OpenClaw sessions.

entries.label: Embedded Pi Project Settings Policy
entries.help: How embedded Pi handles workspace-local `.pi/config/settings.json`: "sanitize" (default) strips shellPath/shellCommandPrefix, "ignore" disables project settings entirely, and "trusted" applies project settings as-is.

entries.label: Envelope Elapsed
entries.help: Include elapsed time in message envelopes ("on" or "off").

entries.label: Envelope Timestamp
entries.help: Include absolute timestamps in message envelopes ("on" or "off").

entries.label: Envelope Timezone
entries.help: Timezone for message envelopes ("utc", "local", "user", or an IANA timezone string).

entries.label: Heartbeat Direct Policy
entries.help: Controls whether heartbeat delivery may target direct/DM chats: "allow" (default) permits DM delivery and "block" suppresses direct-target sends.

entries.label: Heartbeat Suppress Tool Error Warnings
entries.help: Suppress tool error warning payloads during heartbeat runs.

Delivery target ("last", "none", or a channel id). Known channels: telegram, whatsapp, discord, irc, googlechat, slack, signal, imessage, line, bluebubbles, feishu, matrix, mattermost, msteams, nextcloud-talk, nostr, qqbot, synology-chat, tlon, twitch, zalo, zalouser.

entries.label: Human Delay Max (ms)
entries.help: Maximum delay in ms for custom humanDelay (default: 2500).

entries.label: Human Delay Min (ms)
entries.help: Minimum delay in ms for custom humanDelay (default: 800).

entries.label: Human Delay Mode
entries.help: Delay style for block replies ("off", "natural", "custom").

entries.label: Image Generation Model Fallbacks
entries.help: Ordered fallback image-generation models (provider/model).

entries.label: Image Generation Model
entries.help: Optional image-generation model (provider/model) used by the shared image generation capability.

entries.label: Image Max Dimension (px)
entries.help: Max image side length in pixels when sanitizing transcript/tool-result image payloads (default: 1200).

entries.label: Image Model Fallbacks
entries.help: Ordered fallback image models (provider/model).

entries.label: Image Model
entries.help: Optional image model (provider/model) used when the primary model lacks image input.

entries.label: Memory Search
entries.help: Vector search over MEMORY.md and memory/*.md (per-agent overrides supported).

entries.label: Memory Search Embedding Cache
entries.help: Caches computed chunk embeddings in SQLite so reindexing and incremental updates run faster (default: true). Keep this enabled unless investigating cache correctness or minimizing disk usage.

entries.label: Memory Search Embedding Cache Max Entries
entries.help: Sets a best-effort upper bound on cached embeddings kept in SQLite for memory search. Use this when controlling disk growth matters more than peak reindex speed.

entries.label: Memory Chunk Overlap Tokens
entries.help: Token overlap between adjacent memory chunks to preserve context continuity near split boundaries. Use modest overlap to reduce boundary misses without inflating index size too aggressively.

entries.label: Memory Chunk Tokens
entries.help: Chunk size in tokens used when splitting memory sources before embedding/indexing. Increase for broader context per chunk, or lower to improve precision on pinpoint lookups.

entries.label: Enable Memory Search
entries.help: Master toggle for memory search indexing and retrieval behavior on this agent profile. Keep enabled for semantic recall, and disable when you want fully stateless responses.

entries.label: Memory Search Session Index (Experimental)
entries.help: Indexes session transcripts into memory search so responses can reference prior chat turns. Keep this off unless transcript recall is needed, because indexing cost and storage usage both increase.

entries.label: Extra Memory Paths
entries.help: Adds extra directories or .md files to the memory index beyond default memory files. Use this when key reference docs live elsewhere in your repo; when multimodal memory is enabled, matching image/audio files under these paths are also eligible for indexing.

entries.label: Memory Search Fallback
entries.help: Backup provider used when primary embeddings fail: "openai", "gemini", "voyage", "mistral", "ollama", "local", or "none". Set a real fallback for production reliability; use "none" only if you prefer explicit failures.

entries.label: Local Embedding Model Path
entries.help: Specifies the local embedding model source for local memory search, such as a GGUF file path or `hf:` URI. Use this only when provider is `local`, and verify model compatibility before large index rebuilds.

entries.label: Memory Search Model
entries.help: Embedding model override used by the selected memory provider when a non-default model is required. Set this only when you need explicit recall quality/cost tuning beyond provider defaults.

entries.label: Memory Search Multimodal
entries.help: Optional multimodal memory settings for indexing image and audio files from configured extra paths. Keep this off unless your embedding model explicitly supports cross-modal embeddings, and set `memorySearch.fallback` to "none" while it is enabled. Matching files are uploaded to the configured remote embedding provider during indexing.

entries.label: Enable Memory Search Multimodal
entries.help: Enables image/audio memory indexing from extraPaths. This currently requires Gemini embedding-2, keeps the default memory roots Markdown-only, disables memory-search fallback providers, and uploads matching binary content to the configured remote embedding provider.

entries.label: Memory Search Multimodal Max File Bytes
entries.help: Sets the maximum bytes allowed per multimodal file before it is skipped during memory indexing. Use this to cap upload cost and indexing latency, or raise it for short high-quality audio clips.

entries.label: Memory Search Multimodal Modalities
entries.help: Selects which multimodal file types are indexed from extraPaths: "image", "audio", or "all". Keep this narrow to avoid indexing large binary corpora unintentionally.

entries.label: Memory Search Output Dimensionality
entries.help: Gemini embedding-2 only: chooses the output vector size for memory embeddings. Use 768, 1536, or 3072 (default), and expect a full reindex when you change it because stored vector dimensions must stay consistent.

entries.label: Memory Search Provider
entries.help: Selects the embedding backend used to build/query memory vectors: "openai", "gemini", "voyage", "mistral", "ollama", or "local". Keep your most reliable provider here and configure fallback for resilience.

entries.label: Memory Search QMD Collections
entries.help: Use this when one agent should query another agent's transcript collections; QMD-specific extra collections let you opt into cross-agent memory search without flattening everything into one shared namespace.

entries.label: QMD Extra Collections
entries.help: Use this when you need directional transcript search across agents; add collections here to scope QMD recalls without creating a shared global transcript namespace.

entries.label: Memory Search Hybrid Candidate Multiplier
entries.help: Expands the candidate pool before reranking (default: 4). Raise this for better recall on noisy corpora, but expect more compute and slightly slower searches.

entries.label: Memory Search Hybrid
entries.help: Combines BM25 keyword matching with vector similarity for better recall on mixed exact + semantic queries. Keep enabled unless you are isolating ranking behavior for troubleshooting.

entries.label: Memory Search MMR Re-ranking
entries.help: Adds MMR reranking to diversify results and reduce near-duplicate snippets in a single answer window. Enable when recall looks repetitive; keep off for strict score ordering.

entries.label: Memory Search MMR Lambda
entries.help: Sets MMR relevance-vs-diversity balance (0 = most diverse, 1 = most relevant, default: 0.7). Lower values reduce repetition; higher values keep tightly relevant but may duplicate.

entries.label: Memory Search Temporal Decay
entries.help: Applies recency decay so newer memory can outrank older memory when scores are close. Enable when timeliness matters; keep off for timeless reference knowledge.

entries.label: Memory Search Temporal Decay Half-life (Days)
entries.help: Controls how fast older memory loses rank when temporal decay is enabled (half-life in days, default: 30). Lower values prioritize recent context more aggressively.

entries.label: Memory Search Text Weight
entries.help: Controls how strongly BM25 keyword relevance influences hybrid ranking (0-1). Increase for exact-term matching; decrease when semantic matches should rank higher.

entries.label: Memory Search Vector Weight
entries.help: Controls how strongly semantic similarity influences hybrid ranking (0-1). Increase when paraphrase matching matters more than exact terms; decrease for stricter keyword emphasis.

entries.label: Memory Search Max Results
entries.help: Maximum number of memory hits returned from search before downstream reranking and prompt injection. Raise for broader recall, or lower for tighter prompts and faster responses.

entries.label: Memory Search Min Score
entries.help: Minimum relevance score threshold for including memory results in final recall output. Increase to reduce weak/noisy matches, or lower when you need more permissive retrieval.

entries.label: Remote Embedding API Key
entries.help: Supplies a dedicated API key for remote embedding calls used by memory indexing and query-time embeddings. Use this when memory embeddings should use different credentials than global defaults or environment variables.

entries.label: Remote Embedding Base URL
entries.help: Overrides the embedding API endpoint, such as an OpenAI-compatible proxy or custom Gemini base URL. Use this only when routing through your own gateway or vendor endpoint; keep provider defaults otherwise.

entries.label: Remote Batch Concurrency
entries.help: Limits how many embedding batch jobs run at the same time during indexing (default: 2). Increase carefully for faster bulk indexing, but watch provider rate limits and queue errors.

entries.label: Remote Batch Embedding Enabled
entries.help: Enables provider batch APIs for embedding jobs when supported (OpenAI/Gemini), improving throughput on larger index runs. Keep this enabled unless debugging provider batch failures or running very small workloads.

entries.label: Remote Batch Poll Interval (ms)
entries.help: Controls how often the system polls provider APIs for batch job status in milliseconds (default: 2000). Use longer intervals to reduce API chatter, or shorter intervals for faster completion detection.

entries.label: Remote Batch Timeout (min)
entries.help: Sets the maximum wait time for a full embedding batch operation in minutes (default: 60). Increase for very large corpora or slower providers, and lower it to fail fast in automation-heavy flows.

entries.label: Remote Batch Wait for Completion
entries.help: Waits for batch embedding jobs to fully finish before the indexing operation completes. Keep this enabled for deterministic indexing state; disable only if you accept delayed consistency.

entries.label: Remote Embedding Headers
entries.help: Adds custom HTTP headers to remote embedding requests, merged with provider defaults. Use this for proxy auth and tenant routing headers, and keep values minimal to avoid leaking sensitive metadata.

entries.label: Memory Search Sources
entries.help: Chooses which sources are indexed: "memory" reads MEMORY.md + memory files, and "sessions" includes transcript history. Keep ["memory"] unless you need recall from prior chat transcripts.

entries.label: Memory Search Index Path
entries.help: Sets where the SQLite memory index is stored on disk for each agent. Keep the default `~/.openclaw/memory/{agentId}.sqlite` unless you need custom storage placement or backup policy alignment.

entries.label: Memory Search Vector Index
entries.help: Enables the sqlite-vec extension used for vector similarity queries in memory search (default: true). Keep this enabled for normal semantic recall; disable only for debugging or fallback-only operation.

entries.label: Memory Search Vector Extension Path
entries.help: Overrides the auto-discovered sqlite-vec extension library path (`.dylib`, `.so`, or `.dll`). Use this when your runtime cannot find sqlite-vec automatically or you pin a known-good build.

entries.label: Index on Search (Lazy)
entries.help: Uses lazy sync by scheduling reindex on search after content changes are detected. Keep enabled for lower idle overhead, or disable if you require pre-synced indexes before any query.

entries.label: Index on Session Start
entries.help: Triggers a memory index sync when a session starts so early turns see fresh memory content. Keep enabled when startup freshness matters more than initial turn latency.

entries.label: Session Delta Bytes
entries.help: Requires at least this many newly appended bytes before session transcript changes trigger reindex (default: 100000). Increase to reduce frequent small reindexes, or lower for faster transcript freshness.

entries.label: Session Delta Messages
entries.help: Requires at least this many appended transcript messages before reindex is triggered (default: 50). Lower this for near-real-time transcript recall, or raise it to reduce indexing churn.

entries.label: Force Reindex After Compaction
entries.help: Forces a session memory-search reindex after compaction-triggered transcript updates (default: true). Keep enabled when compacted summaries must be immediately searchable, or disable to reduce write-time indexing pressure.

entries.label: Watch Memory Files
entries.help: Watches memory files and schedules index updates from file-change events (chokidar). Enable for near-real-time freshness; disable on very large workspaces if watch churn is too noisy.

entries.label: Memory Watch Debounce (ms)
entries.help: Debounce window in milliseconds for coalescing rapid file-watch events before reindex runs. Increase to reduce churn on frequently-written files, or lower for faster freshness.

entries.label: Model Fallbacks
entries.help: Ordered fallback models (provider/model). Used when the primary model fails.

entries.label: Primary Model
entries.help: Primary model (provider/model).

entries.label: Models
entries.help: Configured model catalog (keys are full provider/model IDs).

entries.label: PDF Max Size (MB)
entries.help: Maximum PDF file size in megabytes for the PDF tool (default: 10).

entries.label: PDF Max Pages
entries.help: Maximum number of PDF pages to process for the PDF tool (default: 20).

entries.label: PDF Model Fallbacks
entries.help: Ordered fallback PDF models (provider/model).

entries.label: PDF Model
entries.help: Optional PDF model (provider/model) for the PDF analysis tool. Defaults to imageModel, then session model.

entries.label: Repo Root
entries.help: Optional repository root shown in the system prompt runtime line (overrides auto-detect).

entries.label: Sandbox Browser CDP Source Port Range
entries.help: Optional CIDR allowlist for container-edge CDP ingress (for example 172.21.0.1/32).

entries.label: Sandbox Browser Network
entries.help: Docker network for sandbox browser containers (default: openclaw-sandbox-browser). Avoid bridge if you need stricter isolation.

entries.label: Sandbox Docker Allow Container Namespace Join
entries.help: DANGEROUS break-glass override that allows sandbox Docker network mode container:<id>. This joins another container namespace and weakens sandbox isolation.

entries.label: Workspace
entries.help: Default workspace path exposed to agent runtime tools for filesystem context and repo-aware behavior. Set this explicitly when running from wrappers so path resolution stays deterministic.

entries.label: Agent List
entries.help: Explicit list of configured agents with IDs and optional overrides for model, tools, identity, and workspace. Keep IDs stable over time so bindings, approvals, and session routing remain deterministic.

entries.label: Agent Fast Mode Default
entries.help: Optional per-agent default for fast mode. Applies when no per-message or session fast-mode override is set.

entries.label: Heartbeat Direct Policy
entries.help: Per-agent override for heartbeat direct/DM delivery policy; use "block" for agents that should only send heartbeat alerts to non-DM destinations.

entries.label: Agent Heartbeat Suppress Tool Error Warnings
entries.help: Suppress tool error warning payloads during heartbeat runs.

Delivery target ("last", "none", or a channel id). Known channels: telegram, whatsapp, discord, irc, googlechat, slack, signal, imessage, line, bluebubbles, feishu, matrix, mattermost, msteams, nextcloud-talk, nostr, qqbot, synology-chat, tlon, twitch, zalo, zalouser.

entries.label: Identity Avatar
entries.help: Agent avatar (workspace-relative path, http(s) URL, or data URI).

entries.label: Agent Reasoning Default
entries.help: Optional per-agent default reasoning visibility (on|off|stream). Applies when no per-message or session reasoning override is set.

entries.label: Agent Runtime
entries.help: Optional runtime descriptor for this agent. Use embedded for default OpenClaw execution or acp for external ACP harness defaults.

entries.label: Agent ACP Runtime
entries.help: ACP runtime defaults for this agent when runtime.type=acp. Binding-level ACP overrides still take precedence per conversation.

entries.label: Agent ACP Harness Agent
entries.help: Optional ACP harness agent id to use for this OpenClaw agent (for example codex, claude, cursor, gemini, openclaw).

entries.label: Agent ACP Backend
entries.help: Optional ACP backend override for this agent's ACP sessions (falls back to global acp.backend).

entries.label: Agent ACP Working Directory
entries.help: Optional default working directory for this agent's ACP sessions.

entries.label: Agent ACP Mode
entries.help: Optional ACP session mode default for this agent (persistent or oneshot).

entries.label: Agent Runtime Type
entries.help: Runtime type for this agent: "embedded" (default OpenClaw runtime) or "acp" (ACP harness defaults).

entries.label: Agent Sandbox Browser CDP Source Port Range
entries.help: Per-agent override for CDP source CIDR allowlist.

entries.label: Agent Sandbox Browser Network
entries.help: Per-agent override for sandbox browser Docker network.

entries.label: Agent Sandbox Docker Allow Container Namespace Join
entries.help: Per-agent DANGEROUS override for container namespace joins in sandbox Docker network mode.

entries.label: Agent Skill Filter
entries.help: Optional allowlist of skills for this agent (omit = all skills; empty = no skills).

entries.label: Agent Thinking Default
entries.help: Optional per-agent default thinking level. Overrides agents.defaults.thinkingDefault for this agent when no per-message or session override is set.

entries.label: Agent Tool Allowlist Additions
entries.help: Per-agent additive allowlist for tools on top of global and profile policy. Keep narrow to avoid accidental privilege expansion on specialized agents.

entries.label: Agent Tool Policy by Provider
entries.help: Per-agent provider-specific tool policy overrides for channel-scoped capability control. Use this when a single agent needs tighter restrictions on one provider than others.

entries.label: Agent Tool Profile
entries.help: Per-agent override for tool profile selection when one agent needs a different capability baseline. Use this sparingly so policy differences across agents stay intentional and reviewable.

entries.label: Approvals
entries.help: Approval routing controls for forwarding exec and plugin approval requests to chat destinations outside the originating session. Keep these disabled unless operators need explicit out-of-band approval visibility.

entries.label: Exec Approval Forwarding
entries.help: Groups exec-approval forwarding behavior including enablement, routing mode, filters, and explicit targets. Configure here when approval prompts must reach operational channels instead of only the origin thread.

entries.label: Approval Agent Filter
entries.help: Optional allowlist of agent IDs eligible for forwarded approvals, for example `["primary", "ops-agent"]`. Use this to limit forwarding blast radius and avoid notifying channels for unrelated agents.

entries.label: Forward Exec Approvals
entries.help: Enables forwarding of exec approval requests to configured delivery destinations (default: false). Keep disabled in low-risk setups and enable only when human approval responders need channel-visible prompts.

entries.label: Approval Forwarding Mode
entries.help: Controls where approval prompts are sent: "session" uses origin chat, "targets" uses configured targets, and "both" sends to both paths. Use "session" as baseline and expand only when operational workflow requires redundancy.

entries.label: Approval Session Filter
entries.help: Optional session-key filters matched as substring or regex-style patterns, for example `["discord:", "^agent:ops:"]`. Use narrow patterns so only intended approval contexts are forwarded to shared destinations.

entries.label: Approval Forwarding Targets
entries.help: Explicit delivery targets used when forwarding mode includes targets, each with channel and destination details. Keep target lists least-privilege and validate each destination before enabling broad forwarding.

entries.label: Approval Target Account ID
entries.help: Optional account selector for multi-account channel setups when approvals must route through a specific account context. Use this only when the target channel has multiple configured identities.

entries.label: Approval Target Channel
entries.help: Channel/provider ID used for forwarded approval delivery, such as discord, slack, or a plugin channel id. Use valid channel IDs only so approvals do not silently fail due to unknown routes.

entries.label: Approval Target Thread ID
entries.help: Optional thread/topic target for channels that support threaded delivery of forwarded approvals. Use this to keep approval traffic contained in operational threads instead of main channels.

entries.label: Approval Target Destination
entries.help: Destination identifier inside the target channel (channel ID, user ID, or thread root depending on provider). Verify semantics per provider because destination format differs across channel integrations.

entries.label: Plugin Approval Forwarding
entries.help: Groups plugin-approval forwarding behavior including enablement, routing mode, filters, and explicit targets. Independent of exec approval forwarding. Configure here when plugin approval prompts must reach operational channels.

entries.label: Plugin Approval Agent Filter
entries.help: Optional allowlist of agent IDs eligible for forwarded plugin approvals, for example `["primary", "ops-agent"]`. Use this to limit forwarding blast radius.

entries.label: Forward Plugin Approvals
entries.help: Enables forwarding of plugin approval requests to configured delivery destinations (default: false). Independent of approvals.exec.enabled.

entries.label: Plugin Approval Forwarding Mode
entries.help: Controls where plugin approval prompts are sent: "session" uses origin chat, "targets" uses configured targets, and "both" sends to both paths.

entries.label: Plugin Approval Session Filter
entries.help: Optional session-key filters matched as substring or regex-style patterns, for example `["discord:", "^agent:ops:"]`. Use narrow patterns so only intended approval contexts are forwarded.

entries.label: Plugin Approval Forwarding Targets
entries.help: Explicit delivery targets used when plugin approval forwarding mode includes targets, each with channel and destination details.

entries.label: Plugin Approval Target Account ID
entries.help: Optional account selector for multi-account channel setups when plugin approvals must route through a specific account context.

entries.label: Plugin Approval Target Channel
entries.help: Channel/provider ID used for forwarded plugin approval delivery, such as discord, slack, or a plugin channel id.

entries.label: Plugin Approval Target Thread ID
entries.help: Optional thread/topic target for channels that support threaded delivery of forwarded plugin approvals.

entries.label: Plugin Approval Target Destination
entries.help: Destination identifier inside the target channel (channel ID, user ID, or thread root depending on provider).

entries.label: Audio
entries.help: Global audio ingestion settings used before higher-level tools process speech or media content. Configure this when you need deterministic transcription behavior for voice notes and clips.

entries.label: Audio Transcription
entries.help: Command-based transcription settings for converting audio files into text before agent handling. Keep a simple, deterministic command path here so failures are easy to diagnose in logs.

entries.label: Audio Transcription Command
entries.help: Executable + args used to transcribe audio (first token must be a safe binary/path), for example `["whisper-cli", "--model", "small", "{input}"]`. Prefer a pinned command so runtime environments behave consistently.

entries.label: Audio Transcription Timeout (sec)
entries.help: Maximum time allowed for the transcription command to finish before it is aborted. Increase this for longer recordings, and keep it tight in latency-sensitive deployments.

entries.label: Auth
entries.help: Authentication profile root used for multi-profile provider credentials and cooldown-based failover ordering. Keep profiles minimal and explicit so automatic failover behavior stays auditable.

entries.label: Auth Cooldowns
entries.help: Cooldown/backoff controls for temporary profile suppression after billing-related failures and retry windows. Use these to prevent rapid re-selection of profiles that are still blocked.

entries.label: Billing Backoff (hours)
entries.help: Base backoff (hours) when a profile fails due to billing/insufficient credits (default: 5).

entries.label: Billing Backoff Overrides
entries.help: Optional per-provider overrides for billing backoff (hours).

entries.label: Billing Backoff Cap (hours)
entries.help: Cap (hours) for billing backoff (default: 24).

entries.label: Failover Window (hours)
entries.help: Failure window (hours) for backoff counters (default: 24).

entries.label: Overloaded Backoff (ms)
entries.help: Fixed delay in milliseconds before retrying an overloaded provider/profile rotation (default: 0).

entries.label: Overloaded Profile Rotations
entries.help: Maximum same-provider auth-profile rotations allowed for overloaded errors before switching to model fallback (default: 1).

entries.label: Rate-Limited Profile Rotations
entries.help: Maximum same-provider auth-profile rotations allowed for rate-limit errors before switching to model fallback (default: 1).

entries.label: Auth Profile Order
entries.help: Ordered auth profile IDs per provider (used for automatic failover).

entries.label: Auth Profiles
entries.help: Named auth profiles (provider + mode + optional email).

entries.label: Bindings
entries.help: Top-level binding rules for routing and persistent ACP conversation ownership. Use type=route for normal routing and type=acp for persistent ACP harness bindings.

entries.label: ACP Binding Overrides
entries.help: Optional per-binding ACP overrides for bindings[].type=acp. This layer overrides agents.list[].runtime.acp defaults for the matched conversation.

entries.label: ACP Binding Backend
entries.help: ACP backend override for this binding (falls back to agent runtime ACP backend, then global acp.backend).

entries.label: ACP Binding Working Directory
entries.help: Working directory override for ACP sessions created from this binding.

entries.label: ACP Binding Label
entries.help: Human-friendly label for ACP status/diagnostics in this bound conversation.

entries.label: ACP Binding Mode
entries.help: ACP session mode override for this binding (persistent or oneshot).

entries.label: Binding Agent ID
entries.help: Target agent ID that receives traffic when the corresponding binding match rule is satisfied. Use valid configured agent IDs only so routing does not fail at runtime.

entries.label: Binding Match Rule
entries.help: Match rule object for deciding when a binding applies, including channel and optional account/peer constraints. Keep rules narrow to avoid accidental agent takeover across contexts.

entries.label: Binding Account ID
entries.help: Optional account selector for multi-account channel setups so the binding applies only to one identity. Use this when account scoping is required for the route and leave unset otherwise.

entries.label: Binding Channel
entries.help: Channel/provider identifier this binding applies to, such as `telegram`, `discord`, or a plugin channel ID. Use the configured channel key exactly so binding evaluation works reliably.

entries.label: Binding Guild ID
entries.help: Optional Discord-style guild/server ID constraint for binding evaluation in multi-server deployments. Use this when the same peer identifiers can appear across different guilds.

entries.label: Binding Peer Match
entries.help: Optional peer matcher for specific conversations including peer kind and peer id. Use this when only one direct/group/channel target should be pinned to an agent.

entries.label: Binding Peer ID
entries.help: Conversation identifier used with peer matching, such as a chat ID, channel ID, or group ID from the provider. Keep this exact to avoid silent non-matches.

entries.label: Binding Peer Kind
entries.help: Peer conversation type: "direct", "group", "channel", or legacy "dm" (deprecated alias for direct). Prefer "direct" for new configs and keep kind aligned with channel semantics.

entries.label: Binding Roles
entries.help: Optional role-based filter list used by providers that attach roles to chat context. Use this to route privileged or operational role traffic to specialized agents.

entries.label: Binding Team ID
entries.help: Optional team/workspace ID constraint used by providers that scope chats under teams. Add this when you need bindings isolated to one workspace context.

entries.label: Binding Type
entries.help: Binding kind. Use "route" (or omit for legacy route entries) for normal routing, and "acp" for persistent ACP conversation bindings.

entries.label: Broadcast
entries.help: Broadcast routing map for sending the same outbound message to multiple peer IDs per source conversation. Keep this minimal and audited because one source can fan out to many destinations.

entries.label: Broadcast Destination List
entries.help: Per-source broadcast destination list where each key is a source peer ID and the value is an array of destination peer IDs. Keep lists intentional to avoid accidental message amplification.

entries.label: Broadcast Strategy
entries.help: Delivery order for broadcast fan-out: "parallel" sends to all targets concurrently, while "sequential" sends one-by-one. Use "parallel" for speed and "sequential" for stricter ordering/backpressure control.

entries.label: Browser
entries.help: Browser runtime controls for local or remote CDP attachment, profile routing, and screenshot/snapshot behavior. Keep defaults unless your automation workflow requires custom browser transport settings.

entries.label: Browser Attach-only Mode
entries.help: Restricts browser mode to attach-only behavior without starting local browser processes. Use this when all browser sessions are externally managed by a remote CDP provider.

entries.label: Browser CDP Port Range Start
entries.help: Starting local CDP port used for auto-allocated browser profile ports. Increase this when host-level port defaults conflict with other local services.

entries.label: Browser CDP URL
entries.help: Remote CDP websocket URL used to attach to an externally managed browser instance. Use this for centralized browser hosts and keep URL access restricted to trusted network paths.

entries.label: Browser Accent Color
entries.help: Default accent color used for browser profile/UI cues where colored identity hints are displayed. Use consistent colors to help operators identify active browser profile context quickly.

entries.label: Browser Default Profile
entries.help: Default browser profile name selected when callers do not explicitly choose a profile. Use a stable low-privilege profile as the default to reduce accidental cross-context state use.

entries.label: Browser Enabled
entries.help: Enables browser capability wiring in the gateway so browser tools and CDP-driven workflows can run. Disable when browser automation is not needed to reduce surface area and startup work.

entries.label: Browser Evaluate Enabled
entries.help: Enables browser-side evaluate helpers for runtime script evaluation capabilities where supported. Keep disabled unless your workflows require evaluate semantics beyond snapshots/navigation.

entries.label: Browser Executable Path
entries.help: Explicit browser executable path when auto-discovery is insufficient for your host environment. Use absolute stable paths so launch behavior stays deterministic across restarts.

entries.label: Browser Headless Mode
entries.help: Forces browser launch in headless mode when the local launcher starts browser instances. Keep headless enabled for server environments and disable only when visible UI debugging is required.

entries.label: Browser No-Sandbox Mode
entries.help: Disables Chromium sandbox isolation flags for environments where sandboxing fails at runtime. Keep this off whenever possible because process isolation protections are reduced.

entries.label: Browser Profiles
entries.help: Named browser profile connection map used for explicit routing to CDP ports or URLs with optional metadata. Keep profile names consistent and avoid overlapping endpoint definitions.

entries.label: Browser Profile Attach-only Mode
entries.help: Per-profile attach-only override that skips local browser launch and only attaches to an existing CDP endpoint. Useful when one profile is externally managed but others are locally launched.

entries.label: Browser Profile CDP Port
entries.help: Per-profile local CDP port used when connecting to browser instances by port instead of URL. Use unique ports per profile to avoid connection collisions.

entries.label: Browser Profile CDP URL
entries.help: Per-profile CDP websocket URL used for explicit remote browser routing by profile name. Use this when profile connections terminate on remote hosts or tunnels.

entries.label: Browser Profile Accent Color
entries.help: Per-profile accent color for visual differentiation in dashboards and browser-related UI hints. Use distinct colors for high-signal operator recognition of active profiles.

entries.label: Browser Profile Driver
entries.help: Per-profile browser driver mode. Use "openclaw" (or legacy "clawd") for CDP-based profiles, or use "existing-session" for host-local Chrome DevTools MCP attachment.

entries.label: Browser Profile User Data Dir
entries.help: Per-profile Chromium user data directory for existing-session attachment through Chrome DevTools MCP. Use this for host-local Brave, Edge, Chromium, or non-default Chrome profiles when the built-in auto-connect path would pick the wrong browser data directory.

entries.label: Remote CDP Handshake Timeout (ms)
entries.help: Timeout in milliseconds for post-connect CDP handshake readiness checks against remote browser targets. Raise this for slow-start remote browsers and lower to fail fast in automation loops.

entries.label: Remote CDP Timeout (ms)
entries.help: Timeout in milliseconds for connecting to a remote CDP endpoint before failing the browser attach attempt. Increase for high-latency tunnels, or lower for faster failure detection.

entries.label: Browser Snapshot Defaults
entries.help: Default snapshot capture configuration used when callers do not provide explicit snapshot options. Tune this for consistent capture behavior across channels and automation paths.

entries.label: Browser Snapshot Mode
entries.help: Default snapshot extraction mode controlling how page content is transformed for agent consumption. Choose the mode that balances readability, fidelity, and token footprint for your workflows.

entries.label: Browser SSRF Policy
entries.help: Server-side request forgery guardrail settings for browser/network fetch paths that could reach internal hosts. Keep restrictive defaults in production and open only explicitly approved targets.

entries.label: Browser Allowed Hostnames
entries.help: Explicit hostname allowlist exceptions for SSRF policy checks on browser/network requests. Keep this list minimal and review entries regularly to avoid stale broad access.

entries.label: Browser Allow Private Network
entries.help: Legacy alias for browser.ssrfPolicy.dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork. Prefer the dangerously-named key so risk intent is explicit.

entries.label: Browser Dangerously Allow Private Network
entries.help: Allows access to private-network address ranges from browser tooling. Default is enabled for trusted-network operator setups; disable to enforce strict public-only resolution checks.

entries.label: Browser Hostname Allowlist
entries.help: Legacy/alternate hostname allowlist field used by SSRF policy consumers for explicit host exceptions. Use stable exact hostnames and avoid wildcard-like broad patterns.

entries.label: Canvas Host
entries.help: Canvas host settings for serving canvas assets and local live-reload behavior used by canvas-enabled workflows. Keep disabled unless canvas-hosted assets are actively used.

entries.label: Canvas Host Enabled
entries.help: Enables the canvas host server process and routes for serving canvas files. Keep disabled when canvas workflows are inactive to reduce exposed local services.

entries.label: Canvas Host Live Reload
entries.help: Enables automatic live-reload behavior for canvas assets during development workflows. Keep disabled in production-like environments where deterministic output is preferred.

entries.label: Canvas Host Port
entries.help: TCP port used by the canvas host HTTP server when canvas hosting is enabled. Choose a non-conflicting port and align firewall/proxy policy accordingly.

entries.label: Canvas Host Root Directory
entries.help: Filesystem root directory served by canvas host for canvas content and static assets. Use a dedicated directory and avoid broad repo roots for least-privilege file exposure.

entries.label: Channels
entries.help: Channel provider configurations plus shared defaults that control access policies, heartbeat visibility, and per-surface behavior. Keep defaults centralized and override per provider only where required.

entries.label: CLI
entries.help: CLI presentation controls for local command output behavior such as banner and tagline style. Use this section to keep startup output aligned with operator preference without changing runtime behavior.

entries.label: CLI Banner
entries.help: CLI startup banner controls for title/version line and tagline style behavior. Keep banner enabled for fast version/context checks, then tune tagline mode to your preferred noise level.

entries.label: CLI Banner Tagline Mode
entries.help: Controls tagline style in the CLI startup banner: "random" (default) picks from the rotating tagline pool, "default" always shows the neutral default tagline, and "off" hides tagline text while keeping the banner version line.

entries.label: Commands
entries.help: Controls chat command surfaces, owner gating, and elevated command access behavior across providers. Keep defaults unless you need stricter operator controls or broader command availability.

entries.label: Command Elevated Access Rules
entries.help: Defines elevated command allow rules by channel and sender for owner-level command surfaces. Use narrow provider-specific identities so privileged commands are not exposed to broad chat audiences.

entries.label: Allow Bash Chat Command
entries.help: Allow bash chat command (`!`; `/bash` alias) to run host shell commands (default: false; requires tools.elevated).

entries.label: Bash Foreground Window (ms)
entries.help: How long bash waits before backgrounding (default: 2000; 0 backgrounds immediately).

entries.label: Allow /config
entries.help: Allow /config chat command to read/write config on disk (default: false).

entries.label: Allow /debug
entries.help: Allow /debug chat command for runtime-only overrides (default: false).

entries.label: Allow /mcp
entries.help: Allow /mcp chat command to manage OpenClaw MCP server config under mcp.servers (default: false).

entries.label: Native Commands
entries.help: Registers native slash/menu commands with channels that support command registration (Discord, Slack, Telegram). Keep enabled for discoverability unless you intentionally run text-only command workflows.

entries.label: Native Skill Commands
entries.help: Registers native skill commands so users can invoke skills directly from provider command menus where supported. Keep aligned with your skill policy so exposed commands match what operators expect.

entries.label: Command Owners
entries.help: Explicit owner allowlist for owner-only tools/commands. Use channel-native IDs (optionally prefixed like "whatsapp:+15551234567"). '*' is ignored.

entries.label: Owner ID Display
entries.help: Controls how owner IDs are rendered in the system prompt. Allowed values: raw, hash. Default: raw.

entries.label: Owner ID Hash Secret
entries.help: Optional secret used to HMAC hash owner IDs when ownerDisplay=hash. Prefer env substitution.

entries.label: Allow /plugins
entries.help: Allow /plugins chat command to list discovered plugins and toggle plugin enablement in config (default: false).

entries.label: Allow Restart
entries.help: Allow /restart and gateway restart tool actions (default: true).

entries.label: Text Commands
entries.help: Enables text-command parsing in chat input in addition to native command surfaces where available. Keep this enabled for compatibility across channels that do not support native command registration.

entries.label: Use Access Groups
entries.help: Enforce access-group allowlists/policies for commands.

entries.label: Cron
entries.help: Global scheduler settings for stored cron jobs, run concurrency, delivery fallback, and run-session retention. Keep defaults unless you are scaling job volume or integrating external webhook receivers.

entries.label: Cron Enabled
entries.help: Enables cron job execution for stored schedules managed by the gateway. Keep enabled for normal reminder/automation flows, and disable only to pause all cron execution without deleting jobs.

entries.label: Cron Max Concurrent Runs
entries.help: Limits how many cron jobs can execute at the same time when multiple schedules fire together. Use lower values to protect CPU/memory under heavy automation load, or raise carefully for higher throughput.

entries.label: Cron Retry Policy
entries.help: Overrides the default retry policy for one-shot jobs when they fail with transient errors (rate limit, overloaded, network, server_error). Omit to use defaults: maxAttempts 3, backoffMs [30000, 60000, 300000], retry all transient types.

entries.label: Cron Retry Backoff (ms)
entries.help: Backoff delays in ms for each retry attempt (default: [30000, 60000, 300000]). Use shorter values for faster retries.

entries.label: Cron Retry Max Attempts
entries.help: Max retries for one-shot jobs on transient errors before permanent disable (default: 3).

entries.label: Cron Retry Error Types
entries.help: Error types to retry: rate_limit, overloaded, network, timeout, server_error. Use to restrict which errors trigger retries; omit to retry all transient types.

entries.label: Cron Run Log Pruning
entries.help: Pruning controls for per-job cron run history files under `cron/runs/<jobId>.jsonl`, including size and line retention.

entries.label: Cron Run Log Keep Lines
entries.help: How many trailing run-log lines to retain when a file exceeds maxBytes (default `2000`). Increase for longer forensic history or lower for smaller disks.

entries.label: Cron Run Log Max Bytes
entries.help: Maximum bytes per cron run-log file before pruning rewrites to the last keepLines entries (for example `2mb`, default `2000000`).

entries.label: Cron Session Retention
entries.help: Controls how long completed cron run sessions are kept before pruning (`24h`, `7d`, `1h30m`, or `false` to disable pruning; default: `24h`). Use shorter retention to reduce storage growth on high-frequency schedules.

entries.label: Cron Store Path
entries.help: Path to the cron job store file used to persist scheduled jobs across restarts. Set an explicit path only when you need custom storage layout, backups, or mounted volumes.

entries.label: Cron Legacy Webhook (Deprecated)
entries.help: Deprecated legacy fallback webhook URL used only for old jobs with `notify=true`. Migrate to per-job delivery using `delivery.mode="webhook"` plus `delivery.to`, and avoid relying on this global field.

entries.label: Cron Webhook Bearer Token
entries.help: Bearer token attached to cron webhook POST deliveries when webhook mode is used. Prefer secret/env substitution and rotate this token regularly if shared webhook endpoints are internet-reachable.

entries.label: Diagnostics
entries.help: Diagnostics controls for targeted tracing, telemetry export, and cache inspection during debugging. Keep baseline diagnostics minimal in production and enable deeper signals only when investigating issues.

entries.label: Cache Trace
entries.help: Cache-trace logging settings for observing cache decisions and payload context in embedded runs. Enable this temporarily for debugging and disable afterward to reduce sensitive log footprint.

entries.label: Cache Trace Enabled
entries.help: Log cache trace snapshots for embedded agent runs (default: false).

entries.label: Cache Trace File Path
entries.help: JSONL output path for cache trace logs (default: $OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/logs/cache-trace.jsonl).

entries.label: Cache Trace Include Messages
entries.help: Include full message payloads in trace output (default: true).

entries.label: Cache Trace Include Prompt
entries.help: Include prompt text in trace output (default: true).

entries.label: Cache Trace Include System
entries.help: Include system prompt in trace output (default: true).

entries.label: Diagnostics Enabled
entries.help: Master toggle for diagnostics instrumentation output in logs and telemetry wiring paths. Keep enabled for normal observability, and disable only in tightly constrained environments.

entries.label: Diagnostics Flags
entries.help: Enable targeted diagnostics logs by flag (e.g. ["telegram.http"]). Supports wildcards like "telegram.*" or "*".

entries.label: OpenTelemetry
entries.help: OpenTelemetry export settings for traces, metrics, and logs emitted by gateway components. Use this when integrating with centralized observability backends and distributed tracing pipelines.

entries.label: OpenTelemetry Enabled
entries.help: Enables OpenTelemetry export pipeline for traces, metrics, and logs based on configured endpoint/protocol settings. Keep disabled unless your collector endpoint and auth are fully configured.

entries.label: OpenTelemetry Endpoint
entries.help: Collector endpoint URL used for OpenTelemetry export transport, including scheme and port. Use a reachable, trusted collector endpoint and monitor ingestion errors after rollout.

entries.label: OpenTelemetry Flush Interval (ms)
entries.help: Interval in milliseconds for periodic telemetry flush from buffers to the collector. Increase to reduce export chatter, or lower for faster visibility during active incident response.

entries.label: OpenTelemetry Headers
entries.help: Additional HTTP/gRPC metadata headers sent with OpenTelemetry export requests, often used for tenant auth or routing. Keep secrets in env-backed values and avoid unnecessary header sprawl.

entries.label: OpenTelemetry Logs Enabled
entries.help: Enable log signal export through OpenTelemetry in addition to local logging sinks. Use this when centralized log correlation is required across services and agents.

entries.label: OpenTelemetry Metrics Enabled
entries.help: Enable metrics signal export to the configured OpenTelemetry collector endpoint. Keep enabled for runtime health dashboards, and disable only if metric volume must be minimized.

entries.label: OpenTelemetry Protocol
entries.help: OTel transport protocol for telemetry export: "http/protobuf" or "grpc" depending on collector support. Use the protocol your observability backend expects to avoid dropped telemetry payloads.

entries.label: OpenTelemetry Trace Sample Rate
entries.help: Trace sampling rate (0-1) controlling how much trace traffic is exported to observability backends. Lower rates reduce overhead/cost, while higher rates improve debugging fidelity.

entries.label: OpenTelemetry Service Name
entries.help: Service name reported in telemetry resource attributes to identify this gateway instance in observability backends. Use stable names so dashboards and alerts remain consistent over deployments.

entries.label: OpenTelemetry Traces Enabled
entries.help: Enable trace signal export to the configured OpenTelemetry collector endpoint. Keep enabled when latency/debug tracing is needed, and disable if you only want metrics/logs.

entries.label: Stuck Session Warning Threshold (ms)
entries.help: Age threshold in milliseconds for emitting stuck-session warnings while a session remains in processing state. Increase for long multi-tool turns to reduce false positives; decrease for faster hang detection.

entries.label: Discovery
entries.help: Service discovery settings for local mDNS advertisement and optional wide-area presence signaling. Keep discovery scoped to expected networks to avoid leaking service metadata.

entries.label: mDNS Discovery
entries.help: mDNS discovery configuration group for local network advertisement and discovery behavior tuning. Keep minimal mode for routine LAN discovery unless extra metadata is required.

entries.label: mDNS Discovery Mode
entries.help: mDNS broadcast mode ("minimal" default, "full" includes cliPath/sshPort, "off" disables mDNS).

entries.label: Wide-area Discovery
entries.help: Wide-area discovery configuration group for exposing discovery signals beyond local-link scopes. Enable only in deployments that intentionally aggregate gateway presence across sites.

entries.label: Wide-area Discovery Domain
entries.help: Optional unicast DNS-SD domain for wide-area discovery, such as openclaw.internal. Use this when you intentionally publish gateway discovery beyond local mDNS scopes.

entries.label: Wide-area Discovery Enabled
entries.help: Enables wide-area discovery signaling when your environment needs non-local gateway discovery. Keep disabled unless cross-network discovery is operationally required.

entries.label: Environment
entries.help: Environment import and override settings used to supply runtime variables to the gateway process. Use this section to control shell-env loading and explicit variable injection behavior.

entries.label: Shell Environment Import
entries.help: Shell environment import controls for loading variables from your login shell during startup. Keep this enabled when you depend on profile-defined secrets or PATH customizations.

entries.label: Shell Environment Import Enabled
entries.help: Enables loading environment variables from the user shell profile during startup initialization. Keep enabled for developer machines, or disable in locked-down service environments with explicit env management.

entries.label: Shell Environment Import Timeout (ms)
entries.help: Maximum time in milliseconds allowed for shell environment resolution before fallback behavior applies. Use tighter timeouts for faster startup, or increase when shell initialization is heavy.

entries.label: Environment Variable Overrides
entries.help: Explicit key/value environment variable overrides merged into runtime process environment for OpenClaw. Use this for deterministic env configuration instead of relying only on shell profile side effects.

entries.label: Gateway
entries.help: Gateway runtime surface for bind mode, auth, control UI, remote transport, and operational safety controls. Keep conservative defaults unless you intentionally expose the gateway beyond trusted local interfaces.

entries.label: Gateway Allow x-real-ip Fallback
entries.help: Enables x-real-ip fallback when x-forwarded-for is missing in proxy scenarios. Keep disabled unless your ingress stack requires this compatibility behavior.

entries.label: Gateway Auth
entries.help: Authentication policy for gateway HTTP/WebSocket access including mode, credentials, trusted-proxy behavior, and rate limiting. Keep auth enabled for every non-loopback deployment.

entries.label: Gateway Auth Allow Tailscale Identity
entries.help: Allows trusted Tailscale identity paths to satisfy gateway auth checks when configured. Use this only when your tailnet identity posture is strong and operator workflows depend on it.

entries.label: Gateway Auth Mode
entries.help: Gateway auth mode: "none", "token", "password", or "trusted-proxy" depending on your edge architecture. Use token/password for direct exposure, and trusted-proxy only behind hardened identity-aware proxies.

entries.label: Gateway Password
entries.help: Required for Tailscale funnel.

entries.label: Gateway Auth Rate Limit
entries.help: Login/auth attempt throttling controls to reduce credential brute-force risk at the gateway boundary. Keep enabled in exposed environments and tune thresholds to your traffic baseline.

entries.label: Gateway Token
entries.help: Required by default for gateway access (unless using Tailscale Serve identity); required for non-loopback binds.

entries.label: Gateway Trusted Proxy Auth
entries.help: Trusted-proxy auth header mapping for upstream identity providers that inject user claims. Use only with known proxy CIDRs and strict header allowlists to prevent spoofed identity headers.

entries.label: Gateway Bind Mode
entries.help: Network bind profile: "auto", "lan", "loopback", "custom", or "tailnet" to control interface exposure. Keep "loopback" or "auto" for safest local operation unless external clients must connect.

entries.label: Gateway Channel Health Check Interval (min)
entries.help: Interval in minutes for automatic channel health probing and status updates. Use lower intervals for faster detection, or higher intervals to reduce periodic probe noise.

entries.label: Gateway Channel Max Restarts Per Hour
entries.help: Maximum number of health-monitor-initiated channel restarts allowed within a rolling one-hour window. Once hit, further restarts are skipped until the window expires. Default: 10.

entries.label: Gateway Channel Stale Event Threshold (min)
entries.help: How many minutes a connected channel can go without receiving any event before the health monitor treats it as a stale socket and triggers a restart. Default: 30.

entries.label: Control UI
entries.help: Control UI hosting settings including enablement, pathing, and browser-origin/auth hardening behavior. Keep UI exposure minimal and pair with strong auth controls before internet-facing deployments.

entries.label: Control UI Allowed Origins
entries.help: Allowed browser origins for Control UI/WebChat websocket connections (full origins only, e.g. https://control.example.com). Required for non-loopback Control UI deployments unless dangerous Host-header fallback is explicitly enabled. Setting ["*"] means allow any browser origin and should be avoided outside tightly controlled local testing.

entries.label: Insecure Control UI Auth Toggle
entries.help: Loosens strict browser auth checks for Control UI when you must run a non-standard setup. Keep this off unless you trust your network and proxy path, because impersonation risk is higher.

entries.label: Control UI Base Path
entries.help: Optional URL prefix where the Control UI is served (e.g. /openclaw).

entries.label: Dangerously Allow Host-Header Origin Fallback
entries.help: DANGEROUS toggle that enables Host-header based origin fallback for Control UI/WebChat websocket checks. This mode is supported when your deployment intentionally relies on Host-header origin policy; explicit gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins remains the recommended hardened default.

entries.label: Dangerously Disable Control UI Device Auth
entries.help: Disables Control UI device identity checks and relies on token/password only. Use only for short-lived debugging on trusted networks, then turn it off immediately.

entries.label: Control UI Enabled
entries.help: Enables serving the gateway Control UI from the gateway HTTP process when true. Keep enabled for local administration, and disable when an external control surface replaces it.

entries.label: Control UI Assets Root
entries.help: Optional filesystem root for Control UI assets (defaults to dist/control-ui).

entries.label: Gateway Custom Bind Host
entries.help: Explicit bind host/IP used when gateway.bind is set to custom for manual interface targeting. Use a precise address and avoid wildcard binds unless external exposure is required.

entries.label: Gateway HTTP API
entries.help: Gateway HTTP API configuration grouping endpoint toggles and transport-facing API exposure controls. Keep only required endpoints enabled to reduce attack surface.

entries.label: Gateway HTTP Endpoints
entries.help: HTTP endpoint feature toggles under the gateway API surface for compatibility routes and optional integrations. Enable endpoints intentionally and monitor access patterns after rollout.

entries.label: OpenAI Chat Completions Endpoint
entries.help: Enable the OpenAI-compatible `POST /v1/chat/completions` endpoint (default: false).

entries.label: OpenAI Chat Completions Image Limits
entries.help: Image fetch/validation controls for OpenAI-compatible `image_url` parts.

entries.label: OpenAI Chat Completions Image MIME Allowlist
entries.help: Allowed MIME types for `image_url` parts (case-insensitive list).

entries.label: OpenAI Chat Completions Allow Image URLs
entries.help: Allow server-side URL fetches for `image_url` parts (default: false; data URIs remain supported). Set this to `false` to disable URL fetching entirely.

entries.label: OpenAI Chat Completions Image Max Bytes
entries.help: Max bytes per fetched/decoded `image_url` image (default: 10MB).

entries.label: OpenAI Chat Completions Image Max Redirects
entries.help: Max HTTP redirects allowed when fetching `image_url` URLs (default: 3).

entries.label: OpenAI Chat Completions Image Timeout (ms)
entries.help: Timeout in milliseconds for `image_url` URL fetches (default: 10000).

entries.label: OpenAI Chat Completions Image URL Allowlist
entries.help: Optional hostname allowlist for `image_url` URL fetches; supports exact hosts and `*.example.com` wildcards. Empty or omitted lists mean no hostname allowlist restriction.

entries.label: OpenAI Chat Completions Max Body Bytes
entries.help: Max request body size in bytes for `/v1/chat/completions` (default: 20MB).

entries.label: OpenAI Chat Completions Max Image Parts
entries.help: Max number of `image_url` parts accepted from the latest user message (default: 8).

entries.label: OpenAI Chat Completions Max Total Image Bytes
entries.help: Max cumulative decoded bytes across all `image_url` parts in one request (default: 20MB).

entries.label: Gateway HTTP Security Headers
entries.help: Optional HTTP response security headers applied by the gateway process itself. Prefer setting these at your reverse proxy when TLS terminates there.

entries.label: Strict Transport Security Header
entries.help: Value for the Strict-Transport-Security response header. Set only on HTTPS origins that you fully control; use false to explicitly disable.

entries.label: Gateway Mode
entries.help: Gateway operation mode: "local" runs channels and agent runtime on this host, while "remote" connects through remote transport. Keep "local" unless you intentionally run a split remote gateway topology.

entries.label: Gateway Node Allowlist (Extra Commands)
entries.help: Extra node.invoke commands to allow beyond the gateway defaults (array of command strings). Enabling dangerous commands here is a security-sensitive override and is flagged by `openclaw security audit`.

entries.label: Gateway Node Browser Mode
entries.help: Node browser routing ("auto" = pick single connected browser node, "manual" = require node param, "off" = disable).

entries.label: Gateway Node Browser Pin
entries.help: Pin browser routing to a specific node id or name (optional).

entries.label: Gateway Node Denylist
entries.help: Node command names to block even if present in node claims or default allowlist (exact command-name matching only, e.g. `system.run`; does not inspect shell text inside that command).

entries.label: Gateway Port
entries.help: TCP port used by the gateway listener for API, control UI, and channel-facing ingress paths. Use a dedicated port and avoid collisions with reverse proxies or local developer services.

entries.label: Gateway Push Delivery
entries.help: Push-delivery settings used by the gateway when it needs to wake or notify paired devices. Configure relay-backed APNs here for official iOS builds; direct APNs auth remains env-based for local/manual builds.

entries.label: Gateway APNs Delivery
entries.help: APNs delivery settings for iOS devices paired to this gateway. Use relay settings for official/TestFlight builds that register through the external push relay.

entries.label: Gateway APNs Relay
entries.help: External relay settings for relay-backed APNs sends. The gateway uses this relay for push.test, wake nudges, and reconnect wakes after a paired official iOS build publishes a relay-backed registration.

entries.label: Gateway APNs Relay Base URL
entries.help: Base HTTPS URL for the external APNs relay service used by official/TestFlight iOS builds. Keep this aligned with the relay URL baked into the iOS build so registration and send traffic hit the same deployment.

entries.label: Gateway APNs Relay Timeout (ms)
entries.help: Timeout in milliseconds for relay send requests from the gateway to the APNs relay (default: 10000). Increase for slower relays or networks, or lower to fail wake attempts faster.

entries.label: Config Reload
entries.help: Live config-reload policy for how edits are applied and when full restarts are triggered. Keep hybrid behavior for safest operational updates unless debugging reload internals.

entries.label: Config Reload Debounce (ms)
entries.help: Debounce window (ms) before applying config changes.

entries.label: Restart Deferral Timeout (ms)
entries.help: Maximum time (ms) to wait for in-flight operations to complete before forcing a SIGUSR1 restart. Default: 300000 (5 minutes). Lower values risk aborting active subagent LLM calls.

entries.label: Config Reload Mode
entries.help: Controls how config edits are applied: "off" ignores live edits, "restart" always restarts, "hot" applies in-process, and "hybrid" tries hot then restarts if required. Keep "hybrid" for safest routine updates.

entries.label: Remote Gateway
entries.help: Remote gateway connection settings for direct or SSH transport when this instance proxies to another runtime host. Use remote mode only when split-host operation is intentionally configured.

entries.label: Remote Gateway Password
entries.help: Password credential used for remote gateway authentication when password mode is enabled. Keep this secret managed externally and avoid plaintext values in committed config.

entries.label: Remote Gateway SSH Identity
entries.help: Optional SSH identity file path (passed to ssh -i).

entries.label: Remote Gateway SSH Target
entries.help: Remote gateway over SSH (tunnels the gateway port to localhost). Format: user@host or user@host:port.

entries.label: Remote Gateway TLS Fingerprint
entries.help: Expected sha256 TLS fingerprint for the remote gateway (pin to avoid MITM).

entries.label: Remote Gateway Token
entries.help: Bearer token used to authenticate this client to a remote gateway in token-auth deployments. Store via secret/env substitution and rotate alongside remote gateway auth changes.

entries.label: Remote Gateway Transport
entries.help: Remote connection transport: "direct" uses configured URL connectivity, while "ssh" tunnels through SSH. Use SSH when you need encrypted tunnel semantics without exposing remote ports.

entries.label: Remote Gateway URL
entries.help: Remote Gateway WebSocket URL (ws:// or wss://).

entries.label: Gateway Tailscale
entries.help: Tailscale integration settings for Serve/Funnel exposure and lifecycle handling on gateway start/exit. Keep off unless your deployment intentionally relies on Tailscale ingress.

entries.label: Gateway Tailscale Mode
entries.help: Tailscale publish mode: "off", "serve", or "funnel" for private or public exposure paths. Use "serve" for tailnet-only access and "funnel" only when public internet reachability is required.

entries.label: Gateway Tailscale Reset on Exit
entries.help: Resets Tailscale Serve/Funnel state on gateway exit to avoid stale published routes after shutdown. Keep enabled unless another controller manages publish lifecycle outside the gateway.

entries.label: Gateway TLS
entries.help: TLS certificate and key settings for terminating HTTPS directly in the gateway process. Use explicit certificates in production and avoid plaintext exposure on untrusted networks.

entries.label: Gateway TLS Auto-Generate Cert
entries.help: Auto-generates a local TLS certificate/key pair when explicit files are not configured. Use only for local/dev setups and replace with real certificates for production traffic.

entries.label: Gateway TLS CA Path
entries.help: Optional CA bundle path for client verification or custom trust-chain requirements at the gateway edge. Use this when private PKI or custom certificate chains are part of deployment.

entries.label: Gateway TLS Certificate Path
entries.help: Filesystem path to the TLS certificate file used by the gateway when TLS is enabled. Use managed certificate paths and keep renewal automation aligned with this location.

entries.label: Gateway TLS Enabled
entries.help: Enables TLS termination at the gateway listener so clients connect over HTTPS/WSS directly. Keep enabled for direct internet exposure or any untrusted network boundary.

entries.label: Gateway TLS Key Path
entries.help: Filesystem path to the TLS private key file used by the gateway when TLS is enabled. Keep this key file permission-restricted and rotate per your security policy.

entries.label: Gateway Tool Exposure Policy
entries.help: Gateway-level tool exposure allow/deny policy that can restrict runtime tool availability independent of agent/tool profiles. Use this for coarse emergency controls and production hardening.

entries.label: Gateway Tool Allowlist
entries.help: Explicit gateway-level tool allowlist when you want a narrow set of tools available at runtime. Use this for locked-down environments where tool scope must be tightly controlled.

entries.label: Gateway Tool Denylist
entries.help: Explicit gateway-level tool denylist to block risky tools even if lower-level policies allow them. Use deny rules for emergency response and defense-in-depth hardening.

entries.label: Gateway Trusted Proxy CIDRs
entries.help: CIDR/IP allowlist of upstream proxies permitted to provide forwarded client identity headers. Keep this list narrow so untrusted hops cannot impersonate users.

entries.label: WebChat History Max Chars
entries.help: Max characters per text field in chat.history responses before truncation (default: 12000).

entries.label: Hooks
entries.help: Inbound webhook automation surface for mapping external events into wake or agent actions in OpenClaw. Keep this locked down with explicit token/session/agent controls before exposing it beyond trusted networks.

entries.label: Hooks Allowed Agent IDs
entries.help: Allowlist of agent IDs that hook mappings are allowed to target when selecting execution agents. Use this to constrain automation events to dedicated service agents and reduce blast radius if a hook token is exposed.

entries.label: Hooks Allowed Session Key Prefixes
entries.help: Allowlist of accepted session-key prefixes for inbound hook requests when caller-provided keys are enabled. Use narrow prefixes to prevent arbitrary session-key injection.

entries.label: Hooks Allow Request Session Key
entries.help: Allows callers to supply a session key in hook requests when true, enabling caller-controlled routing. Keep false unless trusted integrators explicitly need custom session threading.

entries.label: Hooks Default Session Key
entries.help: Fallback session key used for hook deliveries when a request does not provide one through allowed channels. Use a stable but scoped key to avoid mixing unrelated automation conversations.

entries.label: Hooks Enabled
entries.help: Enables the hooks endpoint and mapping execution pipeline for inbound webhook requests. Keep disabled unless you are actively routing external events into the gateway.

entries.label: Gmail Hook
