entries.help: Gmail push integration settings used for Pub/Sub notifications and optional local callback serving. Keep this scoped to dedicated Gmail automation accounts where possible.

entries.label: Gmail Hook Account
entries.help: Google account identifier used for Gmail watch/subscription operations in this hook integration. Use a dedicated automation mailbox account to isolate operational permissions.

entries.label: Gmail Hook Allow Unsafe External Content
entries.help: Allows less-sanitized external Gmail content to pass into processing when enabled. Keep disabled for safer defaults, and enable only for trusted mail streams with controlled transforms.

entries.label: Gmail Hook Callback URL
entries.help: Public callback URL Gmail or intermediaries invoke to deliver notifications into this hook pipeline. Keep this URL protected with token validation and restricted network exposure.

entries.label: Gmail Hook Include Body
entries.help: When true, fetch and include email body content for downstream mapping/agent processing. Keep false unless body text is required, because this increases payload size and sensitivity.

entries.label: Gmail Hook Label
entries.help: Optional Gmail label filter limiting which labeled messages trigger hook events. Keep filters narrow to avoid flooding automations with unrelated inbox traffic.

entries.label: Gmail Hook Max Body Bytes
entries.help: Maximum Gmail payload bytes processed per event when includeBody is enabled. Keep conservative limits to reduce oversized message processing cost and risk.

entries.label: Gmail Hook Model Override
entries.help: Optional model override for Gmail-triggered runs when mailbox automations should use dedicated model behavior. Keep unset to inherit agent defaults unless mailbox tasks need specialization.

entries.label: Gmail Hook Push Token
entries.help: Shared secret token required on Gmail push hook callbacks before processing notifications. Use env substitution and rotate if callback endpoints are exposed externally.

entries.label: Gmail Hook Renew Interval (min)
entries.help: Renewal cadence in minutes for Gmail watch subscriptions to prevent expiration. Set below provider expiration windows and monitor renew failures in logs.

entries.label: Gmail Hook Local Server
entries.help: Local callback server settings block for directly receiving Gmail notifications without a separate ingress layer. Enable only when this process should terminate webhook traffic itself.

entries.label: Gmail Hook Server Bind Address
entries.help: Bind address for the local Gmail callback HTTP server used when serving hooks directly. Keep loopback-only unless external ingress is intentionally required.

entries.label: Gmail Hook Server Path
entries.help: HTTP path on the local Gmail callback server where push notifications are accepted. Keep this consistent with subscription configuration to avoid dropped events.

entries.label: Gmail Hook Server Port
entries.help: Port for the local Gmail callback HTTP server when serve mode is enabled. Use a dedicated port to avoid collisions with gateway/control interfaces.

entries.label: Gmail Hook Subscription
entries.help: Pub/Sub subscription consumed by the gateway to receive Gmail change notifications from the configured topic. Keep subscription ownership clear so multiple consumers do not race unexpectedly.

entries.label: Gmail Hook Tailscale
entries.help: Tailscale exposure configuration block for publishing Gmail callbacks through Serve/Funnel routes. Use private tailnet modes before enabling any public ingress path.

entries.label: Gmail Hook Tailscale Mode
entries.help: Tailscale exposure mode for Gmail callbacks: "off", "serve", or "funnel". Use "serve" for private tailnet delivery and "funnel" only when public internet ingress is required.

entries.label: Gmail Hook Tailscale Path
entries.help: Path published by Tailscale Serve/Funnel for Gmail callback forwarding when enabled. Keep it aligned with Gmail webhook config so requests reach the expected handler.

entries.label: Gmail Hook Tailscale Target
entries.help: Local service target forwarded by Tailscale Serve/Funnel (for example http://127.0.0.1:8787). Use explicit loopback targets to avoid ambiguous routing.

entries.label: Gmail Hook Thinking Override
entries.help: Thinking effort override for Gmail-driven agent runs: "off", "minimal", "low", "medium", or "high". Keep modest defaults for routine inbox automations to control cost and latency.

entries.label: Gmail Hook Pub/Sub Topic
entries.help: Google Pub/Sub topic name used by Gmail watch to publish change notifications for this account. Ensure the topic IAM grants Gmail publish access before enabling watches.

entries.label: Internal Hooks
entries.help: Internal hook runtime settings for bundled/custom event handlers loaded from module paths. Use this for trusted in-process automations and keep handler loading tightly scoped.

entries.label: Internal Hooks Enabled
entries.help: Enables processing for internal hook handlers and configured entries in the internal hook runtime. Keep disabled unless internal hook handlers are intentionally configured.

entries.label: Internal Hook Entries
entries.help: Configured internal hook entry records used to register concrete runtime handlers and metadata. Keep entries explicit and versioned so production behavior is auditable.

entries.label: Internal Hook Handlers
entries.help: List of internal event handlers mapping event names to modules and optional exports. Keep handler definitions explicit so event-to-code routing is auditable.

entries.label: Internal Hook Event
entries.help: Internal event name that triggers this handler module when emitted by the runtime. Use stable event naming conventions to avoid accidental overlap across handlers.

entries.label: Internal Hook Export
entries.help: Optional named export for the internal hook handler function when module default export is not used. Set this when one module ships multiple handler entrypoints.

entries.label: Internal Hook Module
entries.help: Safe relative module path for the internal hook handler implementation loaded at runtime. Keep module files in reviewed directories and avoid dynamic path composition.

entries.label: Internal Hook Install Records
entries.help: Install metadata for internal hook modules, including source and resolved artifacts for repeatable deployments. Use this as operational provenance and avoid manual drift edits.

entries.label: Internal Hook Loader
entries.help: Internal hook loader settings controlling where handler modules are discovered at startup. Use constrained load roots to reduce accidental module conflicts or shadowing.

entries.label: Internal Hook Extra Directories
entries.help: Additional directories searched for internal hook modules beyond default load paths. Keep this minimal and controlled to reduce accidental module shadowing.

entries.label: Hook Mappings
entries.help: Ordered mapping rules that match inbound hook requests and choose wake or agent actions with optional delivery routing. Use specific mappings first to avoid broad pattern rules capturing everything.

entries.label: Hook Mapping Action
entries.help: Mapping action type: "wake" triggers agent wake flow, while "agent" sends directly to agent handling. Use "agent" for immediate execution and "wake" when heartbeat-driven processing is preferred.

entries.label: Hook Mapping Agent ID
entries.help: Target agent ID for mapping execution when action routing should not use defaults. Use dedicated automation agents to isolate webhook behavior from interactive operator sessions.

entries.label: Hook Mapping Allow Unsafe External Content
entries.help: When true, mapping content may include less-sanitized external payload data in generated messages. Keep false by default and enable only for trusted sources with reviewed transform logic.

entries.label: Hook Mapping Delivery Channel
entries.help: Delivery channel override for mapping outputs (for example "last", "telegram", "discord", "slack", "signal", "imessage", or "msteams"). Keep channel overrides explicit to avoid accidental cross-channel sends.

entries.label: Hook Mapping Deliver Reply
entries.help: Controls whether mapping execution results are delivered back to a channel destination versus being processed silently. Disable delivery for background automations that should not post user-facing output.

entries.label: Hook Mapping ID
entries.help: Optional stable identifier for a hook mapping entry used for auditing, troubleshooting, and targeted updates. Use unique IDs so logs and config diffs can reference mappings unambiguously.

entries.label: Hook Mapping Match
entries.help: Grouping object for mapping match predicates such as path and source before action routing is applied. Keep match criteria specific so unrelated webhook traffic does not trigger automations.

entries.label: Hook Mapping Match Path
entries.help: Path match condition for a hook mapping, usually compared against the inbound request path. Use this to split automation behavior by webhook endpoint path families.

entries.label: Hook Mapping Match Source
entries.help: Source match condition for a hook mapping, typically set by trusted upstream metadata or adapter logic. Use stable source identifiers so routing remains deterministic across retries.

entries.label: Hook Mapping Message Template
entries.help: Template for synthesizing structured mapping input into the final message content sent to the target action path. Keep templates deterministic so downstream parsing and behavior remain stable.

entries.label: Hook Mapping Model Override
entries.help: Optional model override for mapping-triggered runs when automation should use a different model than agent defaults. Use this sparingly so behavior remains predictable across mapping executions.

entries.label: Hook Mapping Name
entries.help: Human-readable mapping display name used in diagnostics and operator-facing config UIs. Keep names concise and descriptive so routing intent is obvious during incident review.

entries.label: Hook Mapping Session Key
entries.help: Explicit session key override for mapping-delivered messages to control thread continuity. Use stable scoped keys so repeated events correlate without leaking into unrelated conversations.

entries.label: Hook Mapping Text Template
entries.help: Text-only fallback template used when rich payload rendering is not desired or not supported. Use this to provide a concise, consistent summary string for chat delivery surfaces.

entries.label: Hook Mapping Thinking Override
entries.help: Optional thinking-effort override for mapping-triggered runs to tune latency versus reasoning depth. Keep low or minimal for high-volume hooks unless deeper reasoning is clearly required.

entries.label: Hook Mapping Timeout (sec)
entries.help: Maximum runtime allowed for mapping action execution before timeout handling applies. Use tighter limits for high-volume webhook sources to prevent queue pileups.

entries.label: Hook Mapping Delivery Destination
entries.help: Destination identifier inside the selected channel when mapping replies should route to a fixed target. Verify provider-specific destination formats before enabling production mappings.

entries.label: Hook Mapping Transform
entries.help: Transform configuration block defining module/export preprocessing before mapping action handling. Use transforms only from reviewed code paths and keep behavior deterministic for repeatable automation.

entries.label: Hook Transform Export
entries.help: Named export to invoke from the transform module; defaults to module default export when omitted. Set this when one file hosts multiple transform handlers.

entries.label: Hook Transform Module
entries.help: Relative transform module path loaded from hooks.transformsDir to rewrite incoming payloads before delivery. Keep modules local, reviewed, and free of path traversal patterns.

entries.label: Hook Mapping Wake Mode
entries.help: Wake scheduling mode: "now" wakes immediately, while "next-heartbeat" defers until the next heartbeat cycle. Use deferred mode for lower-priority automations that can tolerate slight delay.

entries.label: Hooks Max Body Bytes
entries.help: Maximum accepted webhook payload size in bytes before the request is rejected. Keep this bounded to reduce abuse risk and protect memory usage under bursty integrations.

entries.label: Hooks Endpoint Path
entries.help: HTTP path used by the hooks endpoint (for example `/hooks`) on the gateway control server. Use a non-guessable path and combine it with token validation for defense in depth.

entries.label: Hooks Presets
entries.help: Named hook preset bundles applied at load time to seed standard mappings and behavior defaults. Keep preset usage explicit so operators can audit which automations are active.

entries.label: Hooks Auth Token
entries.help: Shared bearer token checked by hooks ingress for request authentication before mappings run. Treat holders as full-trust callers for the hook ingress surface, not as a separate non-owner role. Use environment substitution and rotate regularly when webhook endpoints are internet-accessible.

entries.label: Hooks Transforms Directory
entries.help: Base directory for hook transform modules referenced by mapping transform.module paths. Use a controlled repo directory so dynamic imports remain reviewable and predictable.

entries.label: Logging
entries.help: Logging behavior controls for severity, output destinations, formatting, and sensitive-data redaction. Keep levels and redaction strict enough for production while preserving useful diagnostics.

entries.label: Console Log Level
entries.help: Console-specific log threshold: "silent", "fatal", "error", "warn", "info", "debug", or "trace" for terminal output control. Use this to keep local console quieter while retaining richer file logging if needed.

entries.label: Console Log Style
entries.help: Console output format style: "pretty", "compact", or "json" based on operator and ingestion needs. Use json for machine parsing pipelines and pretty/compact for human-first terminal workflows.

entries.label: Log File Path
entries.help: Optional file path for persisted log output in addition to or instead of console logging. Use a managed writable path and align retention/rotation with your operational policy.

entries.label: Log Level
entries.help: Primary log level threshold for runtime logger output: "silent", "fatal", "error", "warn", "info", "debug", or "trace". Keep "info" or "warn" for production, and use debug/trace only during investigation.

entries.label: Custom Redaction Patterns
entries.help: Additional custom redact regex patterns applied to log output before emission/storage. Use this to mask org-specific tokens and identifiers not covered by built-in redaction rules.

entries.label: Sensitive Data Redaction Mode
entries.help: Sensitive redaction mode: "off" disables built-in masking, while "tools" redacts sensitive tool/config payload fields. Keep "tools" in shared logs unless you have isolated secure log sinks.

entries.label: MCP
entries.help: Global MCP server definitions managed by OpenClaw. Embedded Pi and other runtime adapters can consume these servers without storing them inside Pi-owned project settings.

entries.label: MCP Servers
entries.help: Named MCP server definitions. OpenClaw stores them in its own config and runtime adapters decide which transports are supported at execution time.

entries.label: Media
entries.help: Top-level media behavior shared across providers and tools that handle inbound files. Keep defaults unless you need stable filenames for external processing pipelines or longer-lived inbound media retention.

entries.label: Preserve Media Filenames
entries.help: When enabled, uploaded media keeps its original filename instead of a generated temp-safe name. Turn this on when downstream automations depend on stable names, and leave off to reduce accidental filename leakage.

entries.label: Media Retention TTL (hours)
entries.help: Optional retention window in hours for persisted inbound media cleanup across the full media tree. Leave unset to preserve legacy behavior, or set values like 24 (1 day) or 168 (7 days) when you want automatic cleanup.

entries.label: Memory
entries.help: Memory backend configuration (global).

entries.label: Memory Backend
entries.help: Selects the global memory engine: "builtin" uses OpenClaw memory internals, while "qmd" uses the QMD sidecar pipeline. Keep "builtin" unless you intentionally operate QMD.

entries.label: Memory Citations Mode
entries.help: Controls citation visibility in replies: "auto" shows citations when useful, "on" always shows them, and "off" hides them. Keep "auto" for a balanced signal-to-noise default.

entries.label: QMD Binary
entries.help: Sets the executable path for the `qmd` binary used by the QMD backend (default: resolved from PATH). Use an explicit absolute path when multiple qmd installs exist or PATH differs across environments.

entries.label: QMD Include Default Memory
entries.help: Automatically indexes default memory files (MEMORY.md and memory/**/*.md) into QMD collections. Keep enabled unless you want indexing controlled only through explicit custom paths.

entries.label: QMD Max Injected Chars
entries.help: Caps how much QMD text can be injected into one turn across all hits. Use lower values to control prompt bloat and latency; raise only when context is consistently truncated.

entries.label: QMD Max Results
entries.help: Limits how many QMD hits are returned into the agent loop for each recall request (default: 6). Increase for broader recall context, or lower to keep prompts tighter and faster.

entries.label: QMD Max Snippet Chars
entries.help: Caps per-result snippet length extracted from QMD hits in characters (default: 700). Lower this when prompts bloat quickly, and raise only if answers consistently miss key details.

entries.label: QMD Search Timeout (ms)
entries.help: Sets per-query QMD search timeout in milliseconds (default: 4000). Increase for larger indexes or slower environments, and lower to keep request latency bounded.

entries.label: QMD MCPorter
entries.help: Routes QMD work through mcporter (MCP runtime) instead of spawning `qmd` for each call. Use this when cold starts are expensive on large models; keep direct process mode for simpler local setups.

entries.label: QMD MCPorter Enabled
entries.help: Routes QMD through an mcporter daemon instead of spawning qmd per request, reducing cold-start overhead for larger models. Keep disabled unless mcporter is installed and configured.

entries.label: QMD MCPorter Server Name
entries.help: Names the mcporter server target used for QMD calls (default: qmd). Change only when your mcporter setup uses a custom server name for qmd mcp keep-alive.

entries.label: QMD MCPorter Start Daemon
entries.help: Automatically starts the mcporter daemon when mcporter-backed QMD mode is enabled (default: true). Keep enabled unless process lifecycle is managed externally by your service supervisor.

entries.label: QMD Extra Paths
entries.help: Adds custom directories or files to include in QMD indexing, each with an optional name and glob pattern. Use this for project-specific knowledge locations that are outside default memory paths.

entries.label: QMD Surface Scope
entries.help: Defines which sessions/channels are eligible for QMD recall using session.sendPolicy-style rules. Keep default direct-only scope unless you intentionally want cross-chat memory sharing.

entries.label: QMD Search Mode
entries.help: Selects the QMD retrieval path: "query" uses standard query flow, "search" uses search-oriented retrieval, and "vsearch" emphasizes vector retrieval. Keep default unless tuning relevance quality.

entries.label: QMD Search Tool Override
entries.help: Overrides the exact mcporter tool name used for QMD searches while preserving `searchMode` as the semantic retrieval mode. Use this only when your QMD MCP server exposes a custom tool such as `hybrid_search` and keep it unset for the normal built-in tool mapping.

entries.label: QMD Session Indexing
entries.help: Indexes session transcripts into QMD so recall can include prior conversation content (experimental, default: false). Enable only when transcript memory is required and you accept larger index churn.

entries.label: QMD Session Export Directory
entries.help: Overrides where sanitized session exports are written before QMD indexing. Use this when default state storage is constrained or when exports must land on a managed volume.

entries.label: QMD Session Retention (days)
entries.help: Defines how long exported session files are kept before automatic pruning, in days (default: unlimited). Set a finite value for storage hygiene or compliance retention policies.

entries.label: QMD Command Timeout (ms)
entries.help: Sets timeout for QMD maintenance commands such as collection list/add in milliseconds (default: 30000). Increase when running on slower disks or remote filesystems that delay command completion.

entries.label: QMD Update Debounce (ms)
entries.help: Sets the minimum delay between consecutive QMD refresh attempts in milliseconds (default: 15000). Increase this if frequent file changes cause update thrash or unnecessary background load.

entries.label: QMD Embed Interval
entries.help: Sets how often QMD recomputes embeddings (duration string, default: 60m; set 0 to disable periodic embeds). Lower intervals improve freshness but increase embedding workload and cost.

entries.label: QMD Embed Timeout (ms)
entries.help: Sets maximum runtime for each `qmd embed` cycle in milliseconds (default: 120000). Increase for heavier embedding workloads or slower hardware, and lower to fail fast under tight SLAs.

entries.label: QMD Update Interval
entries.help: Sets how often QMD refreshes indexes from source content (duration string, default: 5m). Shorter intervals improve freshness but increase background CPU and I/O.

entries.label: QMD Update on Startup
entries.help: Runs an initial QMD update once during gateway startup (default: true). Keep enabled so recall starts from a fresh baseline; disable only when startup speed is more important than immediate freshness.

entries.label: QMD Update Timeout (ms)
entries.help: Sets maximum runtime for each `qmd update` cycle in milliseconds (default: 120000). Raise this for larger collections; lower it when you want quicker failure detection in automation.

entries.label: QMD Wait for Boot Sync
entries.help: Blocks startup completion until the initial boot-time QMD sync finishes (default: false). Enable when you need fully up-to-date recall before serving traffic, and keep off for faster boot.

entries.label: Messages
entries.help: Message formatting, acknowledgment, queueing, debounce, and status reaction behavior for inbound/outbound chat flows. Use this section when channel responsiveness or message UX needs adjustment.

entries.label: Ack Reaction Emoji
entries.help: Emoji reaction used to acknowledge inbound messages (empty disables).

entries.label: Ack Reaction Scope
entries.help: When to send ack reactions ("group-mentions", "group-all", "direct", "all", "off", "none"). "off"/"none" disables ack reactions entirely.

entries.label: Group Chat Rules
entries.help: Group-message handling controls including mention triggers and history window sizing. Keep mention patterns narrow so group channels do not trigger on every message.

entries.label: Group History Limit
entries.help: Maximum number of prior group messages loaded as context per turn for group sessions. Use higher values for richer continuity, or lower values for faster and cheaper responses.

entries.label: Group Mention Patterns
entries.help: Safe case-insensitive regex patterns used to detect explicit mentions/trigger phrases in group chats. Use precise patterns to reduce false positives in high-volume channels; invalid or unsafe nested-repetition patterns are ignored.

entries.label: Inbound Debounce
entries.help: Direct inbound debounce settings used before queue/turn processing starts. Configure this for provider-specific rapid message bursts from the same sender.

entries.label: Inbound Debounce by Channel (ms)
entries.help: Per-channel inbound debounce overrides keyed by provider id in milliseconds. Use this where some providers send message fragments more aggressively than others.

entries.label: Inbound Message Debounce (ms)
entries.help: Debounce window (ms) for batching rapid inbound messages from the same sender (0 to disable).

entries.label: Inbound Message Prefix
entries.help: Prefix text prepended to inbound user messages before they are handed to the agent runtime. Use this sparingly for channel context markers and keep it stable across sessions.

entries.label: Inbound Queue
entries.help: Inbound message queue strategy used to buffer bursts before processing turns. Tune this for busy channels where sequential processing or batching behavior matters.

entries.label: Queue Mode by Channel
entries.help: Per-channel queue mode overrides keyed by provider id (for example telegram, discord, slack). Use this when one channel’s traffic pattern needs different queue behavior than global defaults.

entries.label: Queue Capacity
entries.help: Maximum number of queued inbound items retained before drop policy applies. Keep caps bounded in noisy channels so memory usage remains predictable.

entries.label: Queue Debounce (ms)
entries.help: Global queue debounce window in milliseconds before processing buffered inbound messages. Use higher values to coalesce rapid bursts, or lower values for reduced response latency.

entries.label: Queue Debounce by Channel (ms)
entries.help: Per-channel debounce overrides for queue behavior keyed by provider id. Use this to tune burst handling independently for chat surfaces with different pacing.

entries.label: Queue Drop Strategy
entries.help: Drop strategy when queue cap is exceeded: "old", "new", or "summarize". Use summarize when preserving intent matters, or old/new when deterministic dropping is preferred.

entries.label: Queue Mode
entries.help: Queue behavior mode: "steer", "followup", "collect", "steer-backlog", "steer+backlog", "queue", or "interrupt". Keep conservative modes unless you intentionally need aggressive interruption/backlog semantics.

entries.label: Remove Ack Reaction After Reply
entries.help: Removes the acknowledgment reaction after final reply delivery when enabled. Keep enabled for cleaner UX in channels where persistent ack reactions create clutter.

entries.label: Outbound Response Prefix
entries.help: Prefix text prepended to outbound assistant replies before sending to channels. Use for lightweight branding/context tags and avoid long prefixes that reduce content density.

entries.label: Status Reactions
entries.help: Lifecycle status reactions that update the emoji on the trigger message as the agent progresses (queued → thinking → tool → done/error).

entries.label: Status Reaction Emojis
entries.help: Override default status reaction emojis. Keys: thinking, compacting, tool, coding, web, done, error, stallSoft, stallHard. Must be valid Telegram reaction emojis.

entries.label: Enable Status Reactions
entries.help: Enable lifecycle status reactions for Telegram. When enabled, the ack reaction becomes the initial 'queued' state and progresses through thinking, tool, done/error automatically. Default: false.

entries.label: Status Reaction Timing
entries.help: Override default timing. Keys: debounceMs (700), stallSoftMs (25000), stallHardMs (60000), doneHoldMs (1500), errorHoldMs (2500).

entries.label: Suppress Tool Error Warnings
entries.help: When true, suppress ⚠️ tool-error warnings from being shown to the user. The agent already sees errors in context and can retry. Default: false.

entries.label: Message Text-to-Speech
entries.help: Text-to-speech policy for reading agent replies aloud on supported voice or audio surfaces. Keep disabled unless voice playback is part of your operator/user workflow.

entries.label: TTS Provider Settings
entries.help: Provider-specific TTS settings keyed by speech provider id. Use this instead of bundled provider-specific top-level keys so speech plugins stay decoupled from core config schema.

entries.label: TTS Provider Config
entries.help: Provider-specific TTS configuration for one speech provider id. Keep fields scoped to the plugin that owns that provider.

entries.label: TTS Provider API Key
entries.help: Provider API key used by that speech provider when its plugin requires authenticated TTS access.

entries.label: Metadata
entries.help: Metadata fields automatically maintained by OpenClaw to record write/version history for this config file. Keep these values system-managed and avoid manual edits unless debugging migration history.

entries.label: Config Last Touched At
entries.help: ISO timestamp of the last config write (auto-set).

entries.label: Config Last Touched Version
entries.help: Auto-set when OpenClaw writes the config.

entries.label: Models
entries.help: Model catalog root for provider definitions, merge/replace behavior, and optional Bedrock discovery integration. Keep provider definitions explicit and validated before relying on production failover paths.

entries.label: Bedrock Model Discovery
entries.help: Automatic AWS Bedrock model discovery settings used to synthesize provider model entries from account visibility. Keep discovery scoped and refresh intervals conservative to reduce API churn.

entries.label: Bedrock Default Context Window
entries.help: Fallback context-window value applied to discovered models when provider metadata lacks explicit limits. Use realistic defaults to avoid oversized prompts that exceed true provider constraints.

entries.label: Bedrock Default Max Tokens
entries.help: Fallback max-token value applied to discovered models without explicit output token limits. Use conservative defaults to reduce truncation surprises and unexpected token spend.

entries.label: Bedrock Discovery Enabled
entries.help: Enables periodic Bedrock model discovery and catalog refresh for Bedrock-backed providers. Keep disabled unless Bedrock is actively used and IAM permissions are correctly configured.

entries.label: Bedrock Discovery Provider Filter
entries.help: Optional provider allowlist filter for Bedrock discovery so only selected providers are refreshed. Use this to limit discovery scope in multi-provider environments.

entries.label: Bedrock Discovery Refresh Interval (s)
entries.help: Refresh cadence for Bedrock discovery polling in seconds to detect newly available models over time. Use longer intervals in production to reduce API cost and control-plane noise.

entries.label: Bedrock Discovery Region
entries.help: AWS region used for Bedrock discovery calls when discovery is enabled for your deployment. Use the region where your Bedrock models are provisioned to avoid empty discovery results.

entries.label: Model Catalog Mode
entries.help: Controls provider catalog behavior: "merge" keeps built-ins and overlays your custom providers, while "replace" uses only your configured providers. In "merge", matching provider IDs preserve non-empty agent models.json baseUrl values, while apiKey values are preserved only when the provider is not SecretRef-managed in current config/auth-profile context; SecretRef-managed providers refresh apiKey from current source markers, and matching model contextWindow/maxTokens use the higher value between explicit and implicit entries.

entries.label: Model Providers
entries.help: Provider map keyed by provider ID containing connection/auth settings and concrete model definitions. Use stable provider keys so references from agents and tooling remain portable across environments.

entries.label: Model Provider API Adapter
entries.help: Provider API adapter selection controlling request/response compatibility handling for model calls. Use the adapter that matches your upstream provider protocol to avoid feature mismatch.

entries.label: Model Provider API Key
entries.help: Provider credential used for API-key based authentication when the provider requires direct key auth. Use secret/env substitution and avoid storing real keys in committed config files.

entries.label: Model Provider Auth Mode
entries.help: Selects provider auth style: "api-key" for API key auth, "token" for bearer token auth, "oauth" for OAuth credentials, and "aws-sdk" for AWS credential resolution. Match this to your provider requirements.

entries.label: Model Provider Authorization Header
entries.help: When true, credentials are sent via the HTTP Authorization header even if alternate auth is possible. Use this only when your provider or proxy explicitly requires Authorization forwarding.

entries.label: Model Provider Base URL
entries.help: Base URL for the provider endpoint used to serve model requests for that provider entry. Use HTTPS endpoints and keep URLs environment-specific through config templating where needed.

entries.label: Model Provider Headers
entries.help: Static HTTP headers merged into provider requests for tenant routing, proxy auth, or custom gateway requirements. Use this sparingly and keep sensitive header values in secrets.

entries.label: Model Provider Inject num_ctx (OpenAI Compat)
entries.help: Controls whether OpenClaw injects `options.num_ctx` for Ollama providers configured with the OpenAI-compatible adapter (`openai-completions`). Default is true. Set false only if your proxy/upstream rejects unknown `options` payload fields.

entries.label: Model Provider Model List
entries.help: Declared model list for a provider including identifiers, metadata, and optional compatibility/cost hints. Keep IDs exact to provider catalog values so selection and fallback resolve correctly.

entries.label: Node Host
entries.help: Node host controls for features exposed from this gateway node to other nodes or clients. Keep defaults unless you intentionally proxy local capabilities across your node network.

entries.label: Node Browser Proxy
entries.help: Groups browser-proxy settings for exposing local browser control through node routing. Enable only when remote node workflows need your local browser profiles.

entries.label: Node Browser Proxy Allowed Profiles
entries.help: Optional allowlist of browser profile names exposed through node proxy routing. Leave empty to preserve the default full profile surface, including profile create/delete routes. When set, OpenClaw enforces least-privilege profile access and blocks persistent profile create/delete through the proxy.

entries.label: Node Browser Proxy Enabled
entries.help: Expose the local browser control server through node proxy routing so remote clients can use this host's browser capabilities. Keep disabled unless remote automation explicitly depends on it.

entries.label: Plugins
entries.help: Plugin system controls for enabling extensions, constraining load scope, configuring entries, and tracking installs. Keep plugin policy explicit and least-privilege in production environments.

entries.label: Plugin Allowlist
entries.help: Optional allowlist of plugin IDs; when set, only listed plugins are eligible to load. Use this to enforce approved extension inventories in controlled environments.

entries.label: Plugin Denylist
entries.help: Optional denylist of plugin IDs that are blocked even if allowlists or paths include them. Use deny rules for emergency rollback and hard blocks on risky plugins.

entries.label: Enable Plugins
entries.help: Enable or disable plugin/extension loading globally during startup and config reload (default: true). Keep enabled only when extension capabilities are required by your deployment.

entries.label: Plugin Entries
entries.help: Per-plugin settings keyed by plugin ID including enablement and plugin-specific runtime configuration payloads. Use this for scoped plugin tuning without changing global loader policy.

entries.label: Plugin Install Records
entries.help: CLI-managed install metadata (used by `openclaw plugins update` to locate install sources).

entries.label: Plugin Install Time
entries.help: ISO timestamp of last install/update.

entries.label: Plugin Install Path
entries.help: Resolved install directory for the installed plugin bundle.

entries.label: Plugin Resolved Integrity
entries.help: Resolved npm dist integrity hash for the fetched artifact (if reported by npm).

entries.label: Plugin Marketplace Name
entries.help: Marketplace display name recorded for marketplace-backed plugin installs (if available).

entries.label: Plugin Marketplace Plugin
entries.help: Plugin entry name inside the source marketplace, used for later updates.

entries.label: Plugin Marketplace Source
entries.help: Original marketplace source used to resolve the install (for example a repo path or Git URL).

entries.label: Plugin Resolution Time
entries.help: ISO timestamp when npm package metadata was last resolved for this install record.

entries.label: Plugin Resolved Package Name
entries.help: Resolved npm package name from the fetched artifact.

entries.label: Plugin Resolved Package Spec
entries.help: Resolved exact npm spec (<name>@<version>) from the fetched artifact.

entries.label: Plugin Resolved Package Version
entries.help: Resolved npm package version from the fetched artifact (useful for non-pinned specs).

entries.label: Plugin Resolved Shasum
entries.help: Resolved npm dist shasum for the fetched artifact (if reported by npm).

entries.label: Plugin Install Source
entries.help: Install source ("npm", "archive", or "path").

entries.label: Plugin Install Source Path
entries.help: Original archive/path used for install (if any).

entries.label: Plugin Install Spec
entries.help: Original npm spec used for install (if source is npm).

entries.label: Plugin Install Version
entries.help: Version recorded at install time (if available).

entries.label: Plugin Loader
entries.help: Plugin loader configuration group for specifying filesystem paths where plugins are discovered. Keep load paths explicit and reviewed to avoid accidental untrusted extension loading.

entries.label: Plugin Load Paths
entries.help: Additional plugin files or directories scanned by the loader beyond built-in defaults. Use dedicated extension directories and avoid broad paths with unrelated executable content.

entries.label: Plugin Slots
entries.help: Selects which plugins own exclusive runtime slots such as memory so only one plugin provides that capability. Use explicit slot ownership to avoid overlapping providers with conflicting behavior.

entries.label: Context Engine Plugin
entries.help: Selects the active context engine plugin by id so one plugin provides context orchestration behavior.

entries.label: Memory Plugin
entries.help: Select the active memory plugin by id, or "none" to disable memory plugins.

entries.label: Session
entries.help: Global session routing, reset, delivery policy, and maintenance controls for conversation history behavior. Keep defaults unless you need stricter isolation, retention, or delivery constraints.

entries.label: Session Agent-to-Agent
entries.help: Groups controls for inter-agent session exchanges, including loop prevention limits on reply chaining. Keep defaults unless you run advanced agent-to-agent automation with strict turn caps.

entries.label: Agent-to-Agent Ping-Pong Turns
entries.help: Max reply-back turns between requester and target agents during agent-to-agent exchanges (0-5). Use lower values to hard-limit chatter loops and preserve predictable run completion.

entries.label: DM Session Scope
entries.help: DM session scoping: "main" keeps continuity, while "per-peer", "per-channel-peer", and "per-account-channel-peer" increase isolation. Use isolated modes for shared inboxes or multi-account deployments.

entries.label: Session Identity Links
entries.help: Maps canonical identities to provider-prefixed peer IDs so equivalent users resolve to one DM thread (example: telegram:123456). Use this when the same human appears across multiple channels or accounts.

entries.label: Session Idle Minutes
entries.help: Applies a legacy idle reset window in minutes for session reuse behavior across inactivity gaps. Use this only for compatibility and prefer structured reset policies under session.reset/session.resetByType.

entries.label: Session Main Key
entries.help: Overrides the canonical main session key used for continuity when dmScope or routing logic points to "main". Use a stable value only if you intentionally need custom session anchoring.

entries.label: Session Maintenance
entries.help: Automatic session-store maintenance controls for pruning age, entry caps, and file rotation behavior. Start in warn mode to observe impact, then enforce once thresholds are tuned.

entries.label: Session Disk High-water Target
entries.help: Target size after disk-budget cleanup (high-water mark). Defaults to 80% of maxDiskBytes; set explicitly for tighter reclaim behavior on constrained disks.

entries.label: Session Max Disk Budget
entries.help: Optional per-agent sessions-directory disk budget (for example `500mb`). Use this to cap session storage per agent; when exceeded, warn mode reports pressure and enforce mode performs oldest-first cleanup.

entries.label: Session Max Entries
entries.help: Caps total session entry count retained in the store to prevent unbounded growth over time. Use lower limits for constrained environments, or higher limits when longer history is required.

entries.label: Session Maintenance Mode
entries.help: Determines whether maintenance policies are only reported ("warn") or actively applied ("enforce"). Keep "warn" during rollout and switch to "enforce" after validating safe thresholds.

entries.label: Session Prune After
entries.help: Removes entries older than this duration (for example `30d` or `12h`) during maintenance passes. Use this as the primary age-retention control and align it with data retention policy.

entries.label: Session Prune Days (Deprecated)
entries.help: Deprecated age-retention field kept for compatibility with legacy configs using day counts. Use session.maintenance.pruneAfter instead so duration syntax and behavior are consistent.

entries.label: Session Reset Archive Retention
entries.help: Retention for reset transcript archives (`*.reset.<timestamp>`). Accepts a duration (for example `30d`), or `false` to disable cleanup. Defaults to pruneAfter so reset artifacts do not grow forever.

entries.label: Session Rotate Size
entries.help: Rotates the session store when file size exceeds a threshold such as `10mb` or `1gb`. Use this to bound single-file growth and keep backup/restore operations manageable.

entries.label: Session Parent Fork Max Tokens
entries.help: Maximum parent-session token count allowed for thread/session inheritance forking. If the parent exceeds this, OpenClaw starts a fresh thread session instead of forking; set 0 to disable this protection.

entries.label: Session Reset Policy
entries.help: Defines the default reset policy object used when no type-specific or channel-specific override applies. Set this first, then layer resetByType or resetByChannel only where behavior must differ.

entries.label: Session Daily Reset Hour
entries.help: Sets local-hour boundary (0-23) for daily reset mode so sessions roll over at predictable times. Use with mode=daily and align to operator timezone expectations for human-readable behavior.

entries.label: Session Reset Idle Minutes
entries.help: Sets inactivity window before reset for idle mode and can also act as secondary guard with daily mode. Use larger values to preserve continuity or smaller values for fresher short-lived threads.

entries.label: Session Reset Mode
entries.help: Selects reset strategy: "daily" resets at a configured hour and "idle" resets after inactivity windows. Keep one clear mode per policy to avoid surprising context turnover patterns.

entries.label: Session Reset by Channel
entries.help: Provides channel-specific reset overrides keyed by provider/channel id for fine-grained behavior control. Use this only when one channel needs exceptional reset behavior beyond type-level policies.

entries.label: Session Reset by Chat Type
entries.help: Overrides reset behavior by chat type (direct, group, thread) when defaults are not sufficient. Use this when group/thread traffic needs different reset cadence than direct messages.

entries.label: Session Reset (Direct)
entries.help: Defines reset policy for direct chats and supersedes the base session.reset configuration for that type. Use this as the canonical direct-message override instead of the legacy dm alias.

entries.label: Session Reset (DM Deprecated Alias)
entries.help: Deprecated alias for direct reset behavior kept for backward compatibility with older configs. Use session.resetByType.direct instead so future tooling and validation remain consistent.

entries.label: Session Reset (Group)
entries.help: Defines reset policy for group chat sessions where continuity and noise patterns differ from DMs. Use shorter idle windows for busy groups if context drift becomes a problem.

entries.label: Session Reset (Thread)
entries.help: Defines reset policy for thread-scoped sessions, including focused channel thread workflows. Use this when thread sessions should expire faster or slower than other chat types.

entries.label: Session Reset Triggers
entries.help: Lists message triggers that force a session reset when matched in inbound content. Use sparingly for explicit reset phrases so context is not dropped unexpectedly during normal conversation.

entries.label: Session Scope
entries.help: Sets base session grouping strategy: "per-sender" isolates by sender and "global" shares one session per channel context. Keep "per-sender" for safer multi-user behavior unless deliberate shared context is required.

entries.label: Session Send Policy
entries.help: Controls cross-session send permissions using allow/deny rules evaluated against channel, chatType, and key prefixes. Use this to fence where session tools can deliver messages in complex environments.

entries.label: Session Send Policy Default Action
entries.help: Sets fallback action when no sendPolicy rule matches: "allow" or "deny". Keep "allow" for simpler setups, or choose "deny" when you require explicit allow rules for every destination.

entries.label: Session Send Policy Rules
entries.help: Ordered allow/deny rules evaluated before the default action, for example `{ action: "deny", match: { channel: "discord" } }`. Put most specific rules first so broad rules do not shadow exceptions.

entries.label: Session Send Rule Action
entries.help: Defines rule decision as "allow" or "deny" when the corresponding match criteria are satisfied. Use deny-first ordering when enforcing strict boundaries with explicit allow exceptions.

entries.label: Session Send Rule Match
entries.help: Defines optional rule match conditions that can combine channel, chatType, and key-prefix constraints. Keep matches narrow so policy intent stays readable and debugging remains straightforward.

entries.label: Session Send Rule Channel
entries.help: Matches rule application to a specific channel/provider id (for example discord, telegram, slack). Use this when one channel should permit or deny delivery independently of others.

entries.label: Session Send Rule Chat Type
entries.help: Matches rule application to chat type (direct, group, thread) so behavior varies by conversation form. Use this when DM and group destinations require different safety boundaries.

entries.label: Session Send Rule Key Prefix
entries.help: Matches a normalized session-key prefix after internal key normalization steps in policy consumers. Use this for general prefix controls, and prefer rawKeyPrefix when exact full-key matching is required.

entries.label: Session Send Rule Raw Key Prefix
entries.help: Matches the raw, unnormalized session-key prefix for exact full-key policy targeting. Use this when normalized keyPrefix is too broad and you need agent-prefixed or transport-specific precision.

entries.label: Session Store Path
entries.help: Sets the session storage file path used to persist session records across restarts. Use an explicit path only when you need custom disk layout, backup routing, or mounted-volume storage.

entries.label: Session Thread Bindings
entries.help: Shared defaults for thread-bound session routing behavior across providers that support thread focus workflows. Configure global defaults here and override per channel only when behavior differs.

entries.label: Thread Binding Enabled
entries.help: Global master switch for thread-bound session routing features and focused thread delivery behavior. Keep enabled for modern thread workflows unless you need to disable thread binding globally.

entries.label: Thread Binding Idle Timeout (hours)
entries.help: Default inactivity window in hours for thread-bound sessions across providers/channels (0 disables idle auto-unfocus). Default: 24.

entries.label: Thread Binding Max Age (hours)
entries.help: Optional hard max age in hours for thread-bound sessions across providers/channels (0 disables hard cap). Default: 0.

entries.label: Session Typing Interval (seconds)
entries.help: Controls interval for repeated typing indicators while replies are being prepared in typing-capable channels. Increase to reduce chatty updates or decrease for more active typing feedback.

entries.label: Session Typing Mode
entries.help: Controls typing behavior timing: "never", "instant", "thinking", or "message" based emission points. Keep conservative modes in high-volume channels to avoid unnecessary typing noise.

entries.label: Watch Skills
entries.help: Enable filesystem watching for skill-definition changes so updates can be applied without full process restart. Keep enabled in development workflows and disable in immutable production images.

entries.label: Skills Watch Debounce (ms)
entries.help: Debounce window in milliseconds for coalescing rapid skill file changes before reload logic runs. Increase to reduce reload churn on frequent writes, or lower for faster edit feedback.

entries.label: Talk
entries.help: Talk-mode voice synthesis settings for voice identity, model selection, output format, and interruption behavior. Use this section to tune human-facing voice UX while controlling latency and cost.

entries.label: Talk API Key
entries.help: Use this legacy ElevenLabs API key for Talk mode only during migration, and keep secrets in env-backed storage. Prefer talk.providers.elevenlabs.apiKey (fallback: ELEVENLABS_API_KEY).

entries.label: Talk Interrupt on Speech
entries.help: If true (default), stop assistant speech when the user starts speaking in Talk mode. Keep enabled for conversational turn-taking.

entries.label: Talk Model ID
entries.help: Legacy ElevenLabs model ID for Talk mode (default: eleven_v3). Prefer talk.providers.elevenlabs.modelId.

entries.label: Talk Output Format
entries.help: Use this legacy ElevenLabs output format for Talk mode (for example pcm_44100 or mp3_44100_128) only during migration. Prefer talk.providers.elevenlabs.outputFormat.

entries.label: Talk Active Provider
entries.help: Active Talk provider id (for example "elevenlabs").

entries.label: Talk Provider Settings
entries.help: Provider-specific Talk settings keyed by provider id. During migration, prefer this over legacy talk.* keys.

entries.label: Talk Provider API Key
entries.help: Provider API key for Talk mode.

entries.label: Talk Provider Model ID
entries.help: Provider default model ID for Talk mode.

entries.label: Talk Provider Output Format
entries.help: Provider default output format for Talk mode.

entries.label: Talk Provider Voice Aliases
entries.help: Optional provider voice alias map for Talk directives.

entries.label: Talk Provider Voice ID
entries.help: Provider default voice ID for Talk mode.

entries.label: Talk Silence Timeout (ms)
entries.help: Milliseconds of user silence before Talk mode finalizes and sends the current transcript. Leave unset to keep the platform default pause window (700 ms on macOS and Android, 900 ms on iOS).

entries.label: Talk Voice Aliases
entries.help: Use this legacy ElevenLabs voice alias map (for example {"Clawd":"EXAVITQu4vr4xnSDxMaL"}) only during migration. Prefer talk.providers.elevenlabs.voiceAliases.

entries.label: Talk Voice ID
entries.help: Legacy ElevenLabs default voice ID for Talk mode. Prefer talk.providers.elevenlabs.voiceId.

entries.label: Tools
entries.help: Global tool access policy and capability configuration across web, exec, media, messaging, and elevated surfaces. Use this section to constrain risky capabilities before broad rollout.

entries.label: Agent-to-Agent Tool Access
entries.help: Policy for allowing agent-to-agent tool calls and constraining which target agents can be reached. Keep disabled or tightly scoped unless cross-agent orchestration is intentionally enabled.

entries.label: Agent-to-Agent Target Allowlist
entries.help: Allowlist of target agent IDs permitted for agent_to_agent calls when orchestration is enabled. Use explicit allowlists to avoid uncontrolled cross-agent call graphs.

entries.label: Enable Agent-to-Agent Tool
entries.help: Enables the agent_to_agent tool surface so one agent can invoke another agent at runtime. Keep off in simple deployments and enable only when orchestration value outweighs complexity.

entries.label: Tool Allowlist
entries.help: Absolute tool allowlist that replaces profile-derived defaults for strict environments. Use this only when you intentionally run a tightly curated subset of tool capabilities.

entries.label: Tool Allowlist Additions
entries.help: Extra tool allowlist entries merged on top of the selected tool profile and default policy. Keep this list small and explicit so audits can quickly identify intentional policy exceptions.

entries.label: Tool Policy by Provider
entries.help: Per-provider tool allow/deny overrides keyed by channel/provider ID to tailor capabilities by surface. Use this when one provider needs stricter controls than global tool policy.

entries.label: Tool Denylist
entries.help: Global tool denylist that blocks listed tools even when profile or provider rules would allow them. Use deny rules for emergency lockouts and long-term defense-in-depth.

entries.label: Elevated Tool Access
entries.help: Elevated tool access controls for privileged command surfaces that should only be reachable from trusted senders. Keep disabled unless operator workflows explicitly require elevated actions.

entries.label: Elevated Tool Allow Rules
entries.help: Sender allow rules for elevated tools, usually keyed by channel/provider identity formats. Use narrow, explicit identities so elevated commands cannot be triggered by unintended users.

entries.label: Enable Elevated Tool Access
entries.help: Enables elevated tool execution path when sender and policy checks pass. Keep disabled in public/shared channels and enable only for trusted owner-operated contexts.

entries.label: Exec Tool
entries.help: Exec-tool policy grouping for shell execution host, security mode, approval behavior, and runtime bindings. Keep conservative defaults in production and tighten elevated execution paths.

entries.label: apply_patch Model Allowlist
entries.help: Optional allowlist of model ids (e.g. "gpt-5.2" or "openai/gpt-5.2").

entries.label: Enable apply_patch
entries.help: Enable or disable apply_patch for OpenAI and OpenAI Codex models when allowed by tool policy (default: true).

entries.label: apply_patch Workspace-Only
entries.help: Restrict apply_patch paths to the workspace directory (default: true). Set false to allow writing outside the workspace (dangerous).

entries.label: Exec Ask
entries.help: Approval strategy for when exec commands require human confirmation before running. Use stricter ask behavior in shared channels and lower-friction settings in private operator contexts.

entries.label: Exec Target
entries.help: Selects execution target strategy for shell commands. Use "auto" for runtime-aware behavior (sandbox when available, otherwise gateway), or pin sandbox/gateway/node explicitly when you need a fixed surface.

entries.label: Exec Node Binding
entries.help: Node binding configuration for exec tooling when command execution is delegated through connected nodes. Use explicit node binding only when multi-node routing is required.

entries.label: Exec Notify On Exit
entries.help: When true (default), backgrounded exec sessions on exit and node exec lifecycle events enqueue a system event and request a heartbeat.

entries.label: Exec Notify On Empty Success
entries.help: When true, successful backgrounded exec exits with empty output still enqueue a completion system event (default: false).

entries.label: Exec PATH Prepend
entries.help: Directories to prepend to PATH for exec runs (gateway/sandbox).

entries.label: Exec Safe Bin Profiles
entries.help: Optional per-binary safe-bin profiles (positional limits + allowed/denied flags).

entries.label: Exec Safe Bins
entries.help: Allow stdin-only safe binaries to run without explicit allowlist entries.

entries.label: Exec Safe Bin Trusted Dirs
entries.help: Additional explicit directories trusted for safe-bin path checks (PATH entries are never auto-trusted).

entries.label: Exec Security
entries.help: Execution security posture selector controlling sandbox/approval expectations for command execution. Keep strict security mode for untrusted prompts and relax only for trusted operator workflows.

entries.label: Require Inline-Eval Approval
entries.help: Require explicit approval for interpreter inline-eval forms such as `python -c`, `node -e`, `ruby -e`, or `osascript -e`. Prevents silent allowlist reuse and downgrades allow-always to ask-each-time for those forms.

entries.label: Workspace-only FS tools
entries.help: Restrict filesystem tools (read/write/edit/apply_patch) to the workspace directory (default: false).

entries.label: Enable Link Understanding
entries.help: Enable automatic link understanding pre-processing so URLs can be summarized before agent reasoning. Keep enabled for richer context, and disable when strict minimal processing is required.

entries.label: Link Understanding Max Links
entries.help: Maximum number of links expanded per turn during link understanding. Use lower values to control latency/cost in chatty threads and higher values when multi-link context is critical.

entries.label: Link Understanding Models
entries.help: Preferred model list for link understanding tasks, evaluated in order as fallbacks when supported. Use lightweight models first for routine summarization and heavier models only when needed.

entries.label: Link Understanding Scope
entries.help: Controls when link understanding runs relative to conversation context and message type. Keep scope conservative to avoid unnecessary fetches on messages where links are not actionable.

entries.label: Link Understanding Timeout (sec)
entries.help: Per-link understanding timeout budget in seconds before unresolved links are skipped. Keep this bounded to avoid long stalls when external sites are slow or unreachable.

entries.label: Tool-loop Critical Threshold
entries.help: Critical threshold for repetitive patterns when detector is enabled (default: 20).

entries.label: Tool-loop Generic Repeat Detection
entries.help: Enable generic repeated same-tool/same-params loop detection (default: true).

entries.label: Tool-loop Poll No-Progress Detection
entries.help: Enable known poll tool no-progress loop detection (default: true).

entries.label: Tool-loop Ping-Pong Detection
entries.help: Enable ping-pong loop detection (default: true).

entries.label: Tool-loop Detection
entries.help: Enable repetitive tool-call loop detection and backoff safety checks (default: false).

entries.label: Tool-loop Global Circuit Breaker Threshold
entries.help: Global no-progress breaker threshold (default: 30).

entries.label: Tool-loop History Size
entries.help: Tool history window size for loop detection (default: 30).

entries.label: Tool-loop Warning Threshold
entries.help: Warning threshold for repetitive patterns when detector is enabled (default: 10).

entries.label: Audio Understanding Attachment Policy
entries.help: Attachment policy for audio inputs indicating which uploaded files are eligible for audio processing. Keep restrictive defaults in mixed-content channels to avoid unintended audio workloads.

entries.label: Transcript Echo Format
entries.help: Format string for the echoed transcript message. Use `{transcript}` as a placeholder for the transcribed text. Default: '📝 "{transcript}"'.

entries.label: Echo Transcript to Chat
entries.help: Echo the audio transcript back to the originating chat before agent processing. When enabled, users immediately see what was heard from their voice note, helping them verify transcription accuracy before the agent acts on it. Default: false.

entries.label: Enable Audio Understanding
entries.help: Enable audio understanding so voice notes or audio clips can be transcribed/summarized for agent context. Disable when audio ingestion is outside policy or unnecessary for your workflows.

entries.label: Audio Understanding Language
entries.help: Preferred language hint for audio understanding/transcription when provider support is available. Set this to improve recognition accuracy for known primary languages.

entries.label: Audio Understanding Max Bytes
entries.help: Maximum accepted audio payload size in bytes before processing is rejected or clipped by policy. Set this based on expected recording length and upstream provider limits.

entries.label: Audio Understanding Max Chars
entries.help: Maximum characters retained from audio understanding output to prevent oversized transcript injection. Increase for long-form dictation, or lower to keep conversational turns compact.

entries.label: Audio Understanding Models
entries.help: Ordered model preferences specifically for audio understanding, used before shared media model fallback. Choose models optimized for transcription quality in your primary language/domain.

entries.label: Audio Understanding Prompt
entries.help: Instruction template guiding audio understanding output style, such as concise summary versus near-verbatim transcript. Keep wording consistent so downstream automations can rely on output format.

entries.label: Audio Understanding Scope
entries.help: Scope selector for when audio understanding runs across inbound messages and attachments. Keep focused scopes in high-volume channels to reduce cost and avoid accidental transcription.

entries.label: Audio Understanding Timeout (sec)
entries.help: Timeout in seconds for audio understanding execution before the operation is cancelled. Use longer timeouts for long recordings and tighter ones for interactive chat responsiveness.

entries.label: Media Understanding Concurrency
entries.help: Maximum number of concurrent media understanding operations per turn across image, audio, and video tasks. Lower this in resource-constrained deployments to prevent CPU/network saturation.

entries.label: Image Understanding Attachment Policy
entries.help: Attachment handling policy for image inputs, including which message attachments qualify for image analysis. Use restrictive settings in untrusted channels to reduce unexpected processing.

entries.label: Enable Image Understanding
entries.help: Enable image understanding so attached or referenced images can be interpreted into textual context. Disable if you need text-only operation or want to avoid image-processing cost.

entries.label: Image Understanding Max Bytes
entries.help: Maximum accepted image payload size in bytes before the item is skipped or truncated by policy. Keep limits realistic for your provider caps and infrastructure bandwidth.

entries.label: Image Understanding Max Chars
entries.help: Maximum characters returned from image understanding output after model response normalization. Use tighter limits to reduce prompt bloat and larger limits for detail-heavy OCR tasks.

entries.label: Image Understanding Models
entries.help: Ordered model preferences specifically for image understanding when you want to override shared media models. Put the most reliable multimodal model first to reduce fallback attempts.

entries.label: Image Understanding Prompt
entries.help: Instruction template used for image understanding requests to shape extraction style and detail level. Keep prompts deterministic so outputs stay consistent across turns and channels.

entries.label: Image Understanding Scope
entries.help: Scope selector for when image understanding is attempted (for example only explicit requests versus broader auto-detection). Keep narrow scope in busy channels to control token and API spend.

entries.label: Image Understanding Timeout (sec)
entries.help: Timeout in seconds for each image understanding request before it is aborted. Increase for high-resolution analysis and lower it for latency-sensitive operator workflows.

entries.label: Media Understanding Shared Models
entries.help: Shared fallback model list used by media understanding tools when modality-specific model lists are not set. Keep this aligned with available multimodal providers to avoid runtime fallback churn.

entries.label: Video Understanding Attachment Policy
entries.help: Attachment eligibility policy for video analysis, defining which message files can trigger video processing. Keep this explicit in shared channels to prevent accidental large media workloads.

entries.label: Enable Video Understanding
entries.help: Enable video understanding so clips can be summarized into text for downstream reasoning and responses. Disable when processing video is out of policy or too expensive for your deployment.

entries.label: Video Understanding Max Bytes
entries.help: Maximum accepted video payload size in bytes before policy rejection or trimming occurs. Tune this to provider and infrastructure limits to avoid repeated timeout/failure loops.

entries.label: Video Understanding Max Chars
entries.help: Maximum characters retained from video understanding output to control prompt growth. Raise for dense scene descriptions and lower when concise summaries are preferred.

entries.label: Video Understanding Models
entries.help: Ordered model preferences specifically for video understanding before shared media fallback applies. Prioritize models with strong multimodal video support to minimize degraded summaries.

entries.label: Video Understanding Prompt
entries.help: Instruction template for video understanding describing desired summary granularity and focus areas. Keep this stable so output quality remains predictable across model/provider fallbacks.

entries.label: Video Understanding Scope
entries.help: Scope selector controlling when video understanding is attempted across incoming events. Narrow scope in noisy channels, and broaden only where video interpretation is core to workflow.

entries.label: Video Understanding Timeout (sec)
entries.help: Timeout in seconds for each video understanding request before cancellation. Use conservative values in interactive channels and longer values for offline or batch-heavy processing.

entries.label: Allow Cross-Context Messaging
entries.help: Legacy override: allow cross-context sends across all providers.

entries.label: Enable Message Broadcast
entries.help: Enable broadcast action (default: true).

entries.label: Allow Cross-Context (Across Providers)
entries.help: Allow sends across different providers (default: false).

entries.label: Allow Cross-Context (Same Provider)
entries.help: Allow sends to other channels within the same provider (default: true).

entries.label: Cross-Context Marker
entries.help: Add a visible origin marker when sending cross-context (default: true).

entries.label: Cross-Context Marker Prefix
entries.help: Text prefix for cross-context markers (supports "{channel}").

entries.label: Cross-Context Marker Suffix
entries.help: Text suffix for cross-context markers (supports "{channel}").

entries.label: Tool Profile
entries.help: Global tool profile name used to select a predefined tool policy baseline before applying allow/deny overrides. Use this for consistent environment posture across agents and keep profile names stable.

entries.label: Sandbox Tool Policy
entries.help: Tool policy wrapper for sandboxed agent executions so sandbox runs can have distinct capability boundaries. Use this to enforce stronger safety in sandbox contexts.

entries.label: Sandbox Tool Allow/Deny Policy
entries.help: Allow/deny tool policy applied when agents run in sandboxed execution environments. Keep policies minimal so sandbox tasks cannot escalate into unnecessary external actions.

entries.label: Session Tools Visibility
entries.help: Controls which sessions can be targeted by sessions_list/sessions_history/sessions_send. ("tree" default = current session + spawned subagent sessions; "self" = only current; "agent" = any session in the current agent id; "all" = any session; cross-agent still requires tools.agentToAgent).

entries.label: Subagent Tool Policy
entries.help: Tool policy wrapper for spawned subagents to restrict or expand tool availability compared to parent defaults. Use this to keep delegated agent capabilities scoped to task intent.

entries.label: Subagent Tool Allow/Deny Policy
entries.help: Allow/deny tool policy applied to spawned subagent runtimes for per-subagent hardening. Keep this narrower than parent scope when subagents run semi-autonomous workflows.

entries.label: Web Tools
entries.help: Web-tool policy grouping for search/fetch providers, limits, and fallback behavior tuning. Keep enabled settings aligned with API key availability and outbound networking policy.

entries.label: Web Fetch Cache TTL (min)
entries.help: Cache TTL in minutes for web_fetch results.

entries.label: Enable Web Fetch Tool
entries.help: Enable the web_fetch tool (lightweight HTTP fetch).

entries.label: Web Fetch Max Chars
entries.help: Max characters returned by web_fetch (truncated).

entries.label: Web Fetch Hard Max Chars
entries.help: Hard cap for web_fetch maxChars (applies to config and tool calls).

entries.label: Web Fetch Max Redirects
entries.help: Maximum redirects allowed for web_fetch (default: 3).

entries.label: Web Fetch Max Download Size (bytes)
entries.help: Max download size before truncation.

entries.label: Web Fetch Provider
entries.help: Web fetch fallback provider id.

entries.label: Web Fetch Readability Extraction
entries.help: Use Readability to extract main content from HTML (fallbacks to basic HTML cleanup).

entries.label: Web Fetch Timeout (sec)
entries.help: Timeout in seconds for web_fetch requests.

entries.label: Web Fetch User-Agent
entries.help: Override User-Agent header for web_fetch requests.

entries.label: Web Search Cache TTL (min)
entries.help: Cache TTL in minutes for web_search results.

entries.label: Enable Web Search Tool
entries.help: Enable managed web_search and optional Codex-native search for eligible models.

entries.label: Web Search Max Results
entries.help: Number of results to return (1-10).

entries.label: Codex Allowed Domains
entries.help: Optional domain allowlist passed to the native Codex web_search tool.

entries.label: Codex Search Context Size
entries.help: Native Codex search context size hint: "low", "medium", or "high".

entries.label: Enable Native Codex Web Search
entries.help: Enable native Codex web search for Codex-capable models.

entries.label: Codex Web Search Mode
entries.help: Native Codex web search mode: "cached" (default) or "live".

entries.label: Web Search Provider
entries.help: Search provider id. Auto-detected from available API keys if omitted.

entries.label: Web Search Timeout (sec)
entries.help: Timeout in seconds for web_search requests.

entries.label: UI
entries.help: UI presentation settings for accenting and assistant identity shown in control surfaces. Use this for branding and readability customization without changing runtime behavior.

entries.label: Assistant Appearance
entries.help: Assistant display identity settings for name and avatar shown in UI surfaces. Keep these values aligned with your operator-facing persona and support expectations.

entries.label: Assistant Avatar
entries.help: Assistant avatar image source used in UI surfaces (URL, path, or data URI depending on runtime support). Use trusted assets and consistent branding dimensions for clean rendering.

entries.label: Assistant Name
entries.help: Display name shown for the assistant in UI views, chat chrome, and status contexts. Keep this stable so operators can reliably identify which assistant persona is active.

entries.label: Accent Color
entries.help: Primary accent color used by UI surfaces for emphasis, badges, and visual identity cues. Use high-contrast values that remain readable across light/dark themes.

entries.label: Updates
entries.help: Update-channel and startup-check behavior for keeping OpenClaw runtime versions current. Use conservative channels in production and more experimental channels only in controlled environments.

entries.label: Auto Update Beta Check Interval (hours)
entries.help: How often beta-channel checks run in hours (default: 1).

entries.label: Auto Update Enabled
entries.help: Enable background auto-update for package installs (default: false).

entries.label: Auto Update Stable Delay (hours)
entries.help: Minimum delay before stable-channel auto-apply starts (default: 6).

entries.label: Auto Update Stable Jitter (hours)
entries.help: Extra stable-channel rollout spread window in hours (default: 12).

entries.label: Update Channel
entries.help: Update channel for git + npm installs ("stable", "beta", or "dev").

entries.label: Update Check on Start
entries.help: Check for npm updates when the gateway starts (default: true).

entries.label: Web Channel
entries.help: Web channel runtime settings for heartbeat and reconnect behavior when operating web-based chat surfaces. Use reconnect values tuned to your network reliability profile and expected uptime needs.

entries.label: Web Channel Enabled
entries.help: Enables the web channel runtime and related websocket lifecycle behavior. Keep disabled when web chat is unused to reduce active connection management overhead.

entries.label: Web Channel Heartbeat Interval (sec)
entries.help: Heartbeat interval in seconds for web channel connectivity and liveness maintenance. Use shorter intervals for faster detection, or longer intervals to reduce keepalive chatter.

entries.label: Web Channel Reconnect Policy
entries.help: Reconnect backoff policy for web channel reconnect attempts after transport failure. Keep bounded retries and jitter tuned to avoid thundering-herd reconnect behavior.

entries.label: Web Reconnect Backoff Factor
entries.help: Exponential backoff multiplier used between reconnect attempts in web channel retry loops. Keep factor above 1 and tune with jitter for stable large-fleet reconnect behavior.

entries.label: Web Reconnect Initial Delay (ms)
entries.help: Initial reconnect delay in milliseconds before the first retry after disconnection. Use modest delays to recover quickly without immediate retry storms.

entries.label: Web Reconnect Jitter
entries.help: Randomization factor (0-1) applied to reconnect delays to desynchronize clients after outage events. Keep non-zero jitter in multi-client deployments to reduce synchronized spikes.

entries.label: Web Reconnect Max Attempts
entries.help: Maximum reconnect attempts before giving up for the current failure sequence (0 means no retries). Use finite caps for controlled failure handling in automation-sensitive environments.

entries.label: Web Reconnect Max Delay (ms)
entries.help: Maximum reconnect backoff cap in milliseconds to bound retry delay growth over repeated failures. Use a reasonable cap so recovery remains timely after prolonged outages.

entries.label: Setup Wizard State
entries.help: Setup wizard state tracking fields that record the most recent guided setup run details. Keep these fields for observability and troubleshooting of setup flows across upgrades.

entries.label: Wizard Last Run Timestamp
entries.help: ISO timestamp for when the setup wizard most recently completed on this host. Use this to confirm setup recency during support and operational audits.

entries.label: Wizard Last Run Command
entries.help: Command invocation recorded for the latest wizard run to preserve execution context. Use this to reproduce setup steps when verifying setup regressions.

entries.label: Wizard Last Run Commit
entries.help: Source commit identifier recorded for the last wizard execution in development builds. Use this to correlate setup behavior with exact source state during debugging.

entries.label: Wizard Last Run Mode
entries.help: Wizard execution mode recorded as "local" or "remote" for the most recent setup flow. Use this to understand whether setup targeted direct local runtime or remote gateway topology.

entries.label: Wizard Last Run Version
entries.help: OpenClaw version recorded at the time of the most recent wizard run on this config. Use this when diagnosing behavior differences across version-to-version setup changes.
